I've been here for 1.5 months now. Everyone is very friendly and nice. The rooms are treated properly. There is also a scale. Despite the delicious food, I use my stay to lose weight. I would like to positively mention the room service by Mr. Arabinda and Ms. Tutumoni. Many lounge areas are filled with the same music over and over again, including in the fitness room, which I can only enjoy with earplugs. I sit at the entrance at breakfast. It's significantly quieter there.

The hotel, of course, does not even reach 4*, although it claims to be all 5*. Disadvantages: on the entire ground floor, where the reception (including the restaurant) stinks of burnt oil so strongly that after 10 minutes the clothes must either be thrown away or washed, they are immediately saturated with this smell, apparently the ventilation cannot cope. The water pressure in the room is very weak, taking a shower is uncomfortable. In the fitness room there are very old exercise machines and treadmills worn out to holes. In the Premium Lounge (entrance is not for everyone), the choice of food is extremely poor, it is tasteless and even strange (watermelon and cucumber canapés - this is the first time I have encountered such game).
Great hospitality by Lovi, Aashna & Ayaz.

The room was clean. The bed is king size and spacious. The non-smoking rooms are clean and comfortable. There is also Japanese food. However, it is not pure Japanese food. It tastes like Nepali. The cooking method is wrong. Since it's a first-class hotel, the cleaning and minibar are also expensive. It feels like twice as much.

Excellent hotel, well-trained staff always available to guests. I must thank the staff for helping to make my stay pleasant. In the restaurant you can also eat good international cuisine with the chef also available for personal needs. I recommend it.
The rooms are not very clean, but not bad. The location is good.

I stayed here for almost two months on a business trip to India. The hotel service is very friendly, the rooms are clean, and the restaurant has dumplings and fried noodles, which are suitable for Chinese tastes. Before leaving, the hotel restaurant even gave me a piece of cake, which was very touching. I will stay here again if I come next time. thank you all!

Of course, it seems that not all rooms are like this, but in the room in the toilet there is glass that goes through the room and the toilet, that is, you can see the toilet from the bedroom and there is a curtain that blocks the view, and okay, if it opened and closed from the toilet BUT, it can be opened from the bedroom , that is, you are sitting in such a toilet and anyone who is sitting in the bedroom can open it and this is extremely uncomfortable if you are not alone, also when they brought the pizza there was no knife, it is of course a little cut and can be cut with a fork, but it would be more convenient with a knife, and lastly The refrigerator is a little small and can accommodate 4 bottles (1 liter). Maximum, of course, if you also have any food that needs a refrigerator,
By the way, when I ordered a burger, there were no buns and I had to eat everything in tasteless ordinary bread, in which you can still smell the eggs very strongly, as for me, the lack of ingredients for food in a 5-star hotel is terrible.
Well, that seems to be all for now.

To be 5 stars it is missing things. In the room it is normal that they do not provide towels or that there is a lack of soap. The food is good but the service needs to improve. If you order 2 dishes (a first and a second) it is normal that they bring them to you at the same time,

It seems to me to be a very good hotel, reception and restaurant with a high level... but it is true that much more thorough cleaning of the rooms is needed to be superior. The pool and gym are very good. The only bad thing is the location to be able to take walks.

I recently stayed at this hotel for over two weeks with my teammates, and we had a very pleasant experience.

**Rooms:** The accommodations were exceptionally well maintained, providing a comfortable and inviting atmosphere throughout our stay.

**Room Service:** The room service was prompt and efficient, consistently meeting our needs and requests.

**Food:** The dining options were delicious, with a wide variety of choices available. We appreciated the diverse menu that catered to different tastes.

Overall, our stay was enjoyable, and I would highly recommend this hotel to others seeking a great experience.

I recently stayed at the Pride Plaza Hotel for two days with my son, and we had a wonderful experience. The location is excellent, offering easy access to nearby attractions. Upon arrival, we were pleasantly surprised with a room upgrade from a Deluxe Room to a King Room. The room was spacious, comfortable, and well-appointed, with a clean and modern washroom.

We opted for the breakfast and dinner package, which was a great value. The breakfast buffet featured live counters and a variety of delicious options, while dinner was equally impressive with a range of well-prepared dishes. The restaurant staff was exceptionally helpful, ensuring we had everything we needed, and the service at the front desk was equally commendable.

The hotel’s pool is not specifically designed for children, but with the use of a float, my little one was able to enjoy it comfortably. The pool area was clean and well-maintained, contributing to our overall positive experience.

Considering the price, Pride Plaza offers amenities and services comparable to those of larger, more well-known hotel brands. Overall, we had a fantastic stay, and I would certainly return in the future.

I stayed here with my family for 3 nights in October 2024. Overall the stay was okay for the price but nothing exceptional.
Check-in - Very slow and cumbersome. The front desk need to speak up a little bit, they almost whisper and don't let you know what is happening with the check-in. Should be a whole lot seamless.  3/5

Rooms: Room was pretty good. The bathroom lights weren't working, we reported it and was fixed immediately. Should there not be a check of all electrical before a room is assigned to a guest? 4/5

Breakfast: Quite a poor selection to start with and made worse by the service. Sat down and waited for someone to serve tea, no one came. Same thing with the omelette station, ordered it and provide the table number but no one brought it over. There were 5-6 people walking around and talking amongst themselves which seems to be norm in every hotel without asking the guests if they need anything. Skip this breakfast. 1/5

Liquor permit - Asked about this and apparently the formalities take about 30 minutes? I don't quite understand why that would be the case. The liquor permit desk is only open between 12 and 7 and one time, I went to inquire around 2:30 PM and there was no one there and no one seemed to know where this person was. 1/5

Check-out - Checked out around 11:30 AM and requested a complimentary ride to the next hotel we were staying at (The Taj) which is less than 3 kms away. The ride was requested around 4 PM and they front desk confirmed that it would be arranged since we had a lot of luggage. I came back at 4 and asked about the ride and there was new staff who apparently had no idea of my request. I talked to one person who listened to me, whispered something inaudible and walked away while trying to call someone. I waited for 5 mins and approached someone else and the same thing happened. I got a little upset at this point and asked the guys to speak up and let me know what's happening instead of just walking away. This went on for another 20 mins. A Pride hotel car arrived and I asked if that car can be used for our drop off and the guy said, no it's booked with someone else while the car was parked outside. Eventually I asked for an update and upon not getting anything remotely useful I booked and Uber. Absolutely useless experience. I wasn't trying to save money, the least you could do is clarify one way or another if I was going to get a car or not. I was more than miffed since my daughter was sick and sleeping in the lobby and the staff see this but I didn't see any urgency at all.

Overall, if you get a very good rate, stay else, there are many better options elsewhere.

I recently stayed at the Radisson Blu Hotel in Ahmedabad and found it to be a solid choice, particularly due to its prime location. Situated conveniently close to major attractions and business districts, it made traveling around the city easy and efficient.

The hotel offers a variety of amenities that enhance the overall experience. The staff were friendly and accommodating, always ready to assist with any requests. The dining options available on-site were enjoyable, providing a decent selection of cuisines to satisfy different tastes.

However, while my stay was pleasant, I felt that some areas could use upgrading to meet the expectations of a hotel in this category. The furnishings and decor in certain areas seemed a bit dated, and a refresh would certainly elevate the guest experience.

Overall, Radisson Blu Hotel is not the absolute best, but it’s a reliable option for travelers looking for comfort and convenience in Ahmedabad. With a little modernization, it has the potential to be an outstanding stay for guests. I would consider returning, especially given its excellent location and satisfactory service.

The hotel service was top-notch, with a warm and welcoming attitude from the moment I arrived. Staff members were consistently helpful and knowledgeable, making me feel valued as a guest.I appreciated the efficiency of the hotel service, from swift check-in procedures to prompt room service. Every request was met with a smile and handled with professionalism, enhancing my overall experience.
The housekeeping staff were exceptional in maintaining the cleanliness and comfort of my room. They paid attention to even the smallest details, ensuring that everything was in perfect order throughout my stay.I was impressed by the friendliness and discretion of the housekeeping staff. They respected my privacy while still providing excellent service, making me feel well-cared for during my time at the hotel.

The hotel service exceeded my expectations,I was thoroughly impressed by the level of personalized attention I received from the hotel staff. They anticipated my needs and were proactive in ensuring that I had everything I required.The housekeeping staff maintained impeccable cleanliness throughout my stay. They were discreet yet thorough in their work, ensuring that my room was always spotless and well-organized.The staff's attention to detail was commendable. Not only did they clean the room to a high standard, but they also took the time to arrange personal items neatly and replenish amenities regularly.

I had a fantastic stay where comfort was a top priority. The accommodations were lavish, providing a truly relaxing environment. The check-in and check-out processes were seamless and efficient. Special thanks to Lavish, Shende, Gaurav, and Nandani, whose exceptional assistance greatly enhanced my experience. They were always available, friendly, and attentive, making sure every need was met. Overall, the service was outstanding and contributed significantly to a memorable stay. I highly recommend this place for anyone seeking both luxury and excellent customer service.

My stay at Radisson Blu Ahmedabad was nothing short of exceptional. From the moment I stepped into the hotel, I was greeted with warmth and hospitality that continued throughout my visit.

The hotel's contemporary design and elegant decor create a welcoming ambiance, while the spacious rooms offer comfort and modern amenities. The bed was incredibly comfortable, ensuring a restful night's sleep, and the panoramic views of the city were breathtaking.

One of the highlights of my stay was the dining experience. The hotel's restaurants offer a diverse range of cuisines, from authentic Indian dishes to international favorites. Each meal was expertly prepared with fresh ingredients and served with impeccable service.

The hotel's facilities are also top-notch. Whether relaxing by the pool, working out in the well-equipped gym, or indulging in a spa treatment, there's something for everyone to enjoy.

What truly sets this hotel apart is its exceptional service. The staff went above and beyond to ensure that every aspect of my stay was perfect, from arranging transportation to providing local recommendations.

Overall, my experience at Radisson Blu Ahmedabad was nothing short of outstanding. Whether traveling for business or leisure, I highly recommend this hotel for a luxurious and memorable stay in Ahmedabad.

I recently had the pleasure of staying at the Radisson Hotel in Ahmedabad, and I must say, it was an unforgettable experience from start to finish. From the moment I stepped into the lobby to the day of my departure, every aspect of my stay exceeded expectations.

**Front Office:**
The check-in process was seamless and efficient, thanks to the welcoming and professional staff at the front desk. They were attentive to my needs and ensured that all my preferences were accommodated throughout my stay.

**Staff:**
The staff at Radisson Hotel Ahmedabad truly went above and beyond to make my stay enjoyable. From the housekeeping team who kept my room immaculate to the restaurant staff who provided excellent service during meals, everyone was friendly, courteous, and attentive.

**Services:**
The hotel's services were top-notch in every way. Whether I needed assistance with arranging transportation, booking a tour, or simply seeking recommendations for local attractions, the concierge and guest services team were always ready to assist with a smile.

**Food:**
One of the highlights of my stay was the exceptional dining experience at the hotel's restaurants. The variety and quality of the cuisine surpassed my expectations, and each meal was a delight for the senses. From the lavish breakfast buffet to the delectable dinner options, every dish was expertly prepared and served with care.

Overall, my stay at Radisson Hotel Ahmedabad was nothing short of excellent. The combination of luxurious accommodations, impeccable service, and delicious dining options made it a truly memorable experience. I cannot recommend this hotel highly enough to anyone visiting Ahmedabad.

My recent stay at Taj Hotel was a mix of highs and lows that left a lasting impression. The service throughout the hotel was exceptional, with a welcoming ambiance that exuded luxury. However, the experience took an unexpected turn upon approaching the counter.

Despite this initial hiccup, the overall experience was nothing short of amazing. The rooms, in particular, offered breathtaking views that surpassed expectations. The blend of comfort and aesthetics made the stay truly memorable.

In the end, Taj Hotel's brilliance shone through, and the staff's warmth compensated for the lackluster counter experience. It serves as a reminder that even in the most esteemed establishments, there can be room for improvement. Nevertheless, the captivating views and overall excellence ensured a remarkable stay at Taj Hotel.

Taj Ahmedabad doesn't stand to its brand legacy at all. We had plenty of unpleasant experiences. Booked 2 separate rooms with 6 nights. Check-in was cumbersome. They didn't allow our 12 seater tempo on the 1st floor ramp/main entrance. The head clearance can accommodate a double-decker bus or a volvo bus. But they didn't allow it and, instead,  diverted us to the ground floor entrance that was locked. The employee asked us to go through the outside stairs. We had a lot of carry-on baggage. Suitcases were handled by bell man. During check-in, the concierge insisted on seeing the airport entry stamp on our Canadian passport. We had saved all our IDs on the cellphone before flying, and the physical passport was at the relative's place. It took a while to convince him. No other hotel asks for this type of thing. Then he asked for full payment upfront for all 6 nights. I hesistated since never ever I came across such a compulsion. But had no option...paid full amount of Rs 1.33 lacs upfront. Then he lined us all in the lobby area and took our individual pictures for security reasons. All of us were utterly disappointed. We told him that this policy treats us like criminals. No other hotels have ever asked us for live pictures. They had all our IDs, and there were lots of CCTV cameras around. With our physical presence, why in the world would you embarrass your guests with such a shameless act? Highly unacceptable. The elevators and lobby  area had a typical stinking pungent smell, and we experienced the smell all 6 days. Also, we didn't have breakfast on the first 2 mornings. It was a nightmare. Fish market on the 2nd floor elevator lobby. There was no space to even get out of the elevator and stand for waiting, which was 30 minutes. As a matter of fact, we never had breakfast at all for all 6 days during our stay. The first 2 days were overcrowded. For the last 4 days, my family pushed me to fire their breakfast offer and do it elsewhere since the wait/hassle/smell, etc, was intolerable. When the hotel has several banquets and dining space and when they know that their rooms are fully occupied and its going to be a big rush for breakfast, then why don't they have a plan B in place to accommodate such a huge volumes of guests for breakfast? They could have arranged 2 separate spaces or a bigger space.???

Even they didn't missed disaaponting us during the checkout. I waited fairly in line for my turn far from the desk. When my turn came, the lady at the desk began helping me. I was paying for my room service bill with my credit card. All of a sudden, another customer approached the lady and stood beside me shoulder to shoulder touching me. I had to move a bit. The said customer started talking to the lady about his checkout. To my surprise, the lady started working on his invoice, leaving beside my invoice. I sharply snapped at the lady and asked her to return back to my page on her computer. She promptly printed my invoice and checked me out. Not to mention that neither did she apologize nor did she ask the other customer to wait in line for his turn. What about customer privacy ? The desk staff needs to be properly trained.

Staff is good and courteous , Rooms size is good. Bathrooms compare to other Taj properties were smaller. Ordered Room dining and food was really good and service was fast. Got a follow up call as well from room dining to ensure everything was good. Overall good stay.

Wonderful Hotel.
Best place for corporate events.
Wonderful staff/amenities/ambience.

We checked in on 27th evening..expecting a good stay..but morning breakfast was something really unexpected and insulting..not out first time to stay in taj..dis was our 6th visit here and we dont think we would be coming 7th time..due to our dietary requirements we asked to pack few items and we were denied saying we can’t carry dis much..

We were denied by Mr Rakesh..
When we asked for manager we were attended by Mr Raushan..
Also to mention der was no hygiene maintained,dustbin were touched and without any hygiene measures Dey served food to guest..
Excellent Stay , I would recommend to improve the breakfast buffet menu.

Worst thing ,no one will co operate me
,staf are very rude .
they are say u give 5 star sir .

Do yourself a favor and visit this lovely restaurant in Taj Skyline Hotel Ahmedabad. The service is unmatched. The staff truly care about your experience. The food by Chef Niraj is absolutely amazing and I have to say this everything we tasted melted in other mouths. Highly recommend!
Nice service and elegant rooms with beautiful views of city

Taj Bengal truly exemplifies the epitome of luxury and hospitality. The impeccable service, exquisite dining options, and the opulent ambiance make every stay memorable. From the moment you step inside, you're greeted with warmth and a commitment to excellence. It's a place where every detail is carefully curated to ensure a delightful experience. If your living in Ahmedabad, you must try one time experience with Taj Skyline with your family, your friends and your loved ones. You feel amazed so visit this beautiful Taj asap.

This Taj property sits at a convenient distance from both the airport as well as the main sights of Ahmedabad. Welcome drinks, teeka, and a fragrant tulsi garland welcomed us to the hotel, and check in took only a couple of minutes. Our room was a non-smoking one, well-appointed with mini bar, ironing board and iron, safe, flatscreen TV, day bed, desk and chair, etc. Everything was very clean and well-maintained, and though the view outside our window was boring (lots of high-rise buildings, some under construction), at least we had a very comfortable room, and a good night's rest.

The hotel offers all the usual amenities of a property like this: business centre, restaurants, coffee shop, gym and spa, swimming pool, travel desk, a small shopping arcade (even an astrologer has an office here!) and so on. Overall, helpful and professional staff, and a pleasant place to stay.

Went on a dinner to celebrate a birthday. The ambience and decor is good. The spread is decent, not too many things. But the choice of food and menu was very good and so was the preparation.
An extra Star for the service and care by the chef Nitin & the team.

I would be happy to visit again. I highly recommend it to others as well.
The taj skyline was a very clean hotel with fantastic staff eager to serve.
Taj skyline is wonderful restorent and good cleaning and time to time service and food is good quality and friendly staff
The rooms are very good and the food and other complimentary nice
Amazing hospitality! Attended Raksha Bandhan Special lunch and it was lavish and exotic!

Taj skyline is one of the best hotel I have ever stayed in my life.
The ambiance of the taj is wonderful we you are going to enter in it they will greet you with welcome drink and a mala which is the tradition of taj.

To go to your room you have to scan the room key in lift to access the floor and the view from each room is amazing if you have room from where you can watch the shoot it will be amazing because you can watch go carting from that rooms and can feel like having flat near f1 track just kidding but it's amazing 😍🤩.

I must say if you have good budget just go with taj skyline 👍🏻👍🏻

We had one of the worst experience with Taj skyline Ahmedabad.
We had hosted birthday party of my nephew and sister-in-law there and we are highly disappointed with their service. The staff there does not know what hospitality means and how one should take with the customer.
They keep on passing the call over others. In the era of digital we have been facing to connect with them over the call or message.
The staff over there is least interested in the issues of the customer and resolving them.
Taj... The name itself is enough to describe the elegance of everything that it has to offer. The beautiful experience we had will always want us to revisit this place and the food was awesome.

It's was good ..food was ohkie, but price was not reasonable. Its like 3 star restaurant but charges you thinking they are 4 star. Tables are not clean, floor was bit dirty, though staff was actually good and helpful.

One of the best hotels I have ever visited. Excellent ambience & hospitality. The staff was very friendly and always carried smiles on their faces. The hotel was very clean and tidy. The room which I was given was luxurious and super clean. Also they had such amazing food which I loved to the core.  I enjoyed the stay leisurely.

We had a best experience in taj hotel  staff are very friendly and helpful Mr Kamran Lalit Fahd Urvi treeja and damta it’s was best place to send a time with friends and family we really like the ambiance its very beautiful we love the place very good choice of menu . We had a excellent coffee and nice tea and really like the tea setup  😍

Taj Skyline is a gem in Ahmedabad's culinary scene. It not only offers a gastronomic journey but also provides an ambiance that makes every visit memorable. If you're looking for a refined dining experience with exceptional food, impeccable service, and breathtaking views, Taj Skyline is an absolute must-visit.

The room is clean n quiet, would not be disturbed by the noise on the streets at nights,
Breakfast/dinner is awesome, lots of the different choices/dishes offered and the staff keeps coming asking whether they can offer anything else
Coffee and omelette orders can be taken by the table, sweet
The major minus point is their room wifi, won't work in the 2 days I stay here, even after I called the desk, they said they will work it out but eventually no one showed up and fixed it. Disappointed
The air quality in this area is not good due the dust flying up by the vehicles

Taj Skyline Ahmedabad is a luxurious and contemporary hotel that stands as a testament to both modern architecture and warm Indian hospitality. Located in the vibrant city of Ahmedabad, it offers a stylish retreat for travelers and locals alike.

With its well-appointed rooms, world-class dining options, and impeccable service, Taj Skyline Ahmedabad provides a comfortable and memorable stay experience. The hotel's rooftop bar and restaurant offer stunning views of the city skyline, making it a perfect spot for both relaxation and celebration.

Whether you're in Ahmedabad for business or leisure, Taj Skyline Ahmedabad promises an unforgettable stay where you can savor the flavors of Gujarat and experience the city's dynamic culture in style and comfort.
Luxurious place with truly amazing experience. The food, the rooms, the service and hospitality is fabulous. The staff here are the most hospitable specially Chef Neeraj.

I loved staying at the Taj. I am not from India but this is by far the best hotel I have stayed at. The people in the front at the lobby and outside were so kind and always greeted us with a smile. Breakfast was amazing everyday and the servers were so considerate about everything including providing almond milk for our coffees. Rooms were super clean and the service was unmatched. I will return if I ever come back to the city. I loved everyone and everything about this hotel!

Sharing one of the worst experiences with Taj Skyline, Ahmedabad !Unfortunately, the experience fell far below the standards I would expect from a renowned establishment such as yours.

Firstly, I found the staff to be non-cooperative and unresponsive to customer needs.
Despite being called 20 times , i havent got my refund that i was supposed to get 15 days ago plus whenever you call at hotel and ask about the refund , everytime they say we will call you back !

Staying at Taj is always an EXPERIENCE. It boasts of a grandeur Lobby and Waiting area. The Artifacts and Paimtings are just awesome. There are 3-4 in house Tata Brand outlets at the Lobby area. The rooms are spacious and has all amenities and services. The Normal rooms are so big that three adults can accomodate on bed and at least  6 adults in the room (But They Will Allow only Two). The view of the city from the top is "like a wow".
The Swimming pool is at the 2nd floor and is very small. Although the view from there (of the city) looks amazing.
Service of the Lobby/Reception Staff is doesnt trat everyone equally. My perception says that they are more courteos, generous and special serviceable towards moredrn/rich looking guests and prefer to treat low (if not at all ignore) to middle class guests (here i mean not very rich).
Complimenatry bearkfast was good, but has more of Breads and other items . Although a wide range of Breakfast looked amazing (includes 6-7 types of natural juices, 4-5 Exotic fruits, 2-3 range of Milk, Custard, Yogurt, 4-5 kinds of corn flakes, oats etc, 5-6 types of appetizers, PureHoney directly from honeycomb. Almost everything looked veg excpet 3-4 items of non veg. On Request Chef also made his specialities. The Dinin space was good, has a playing area for the kids and small roof also.
Location is a bit far from township. It is on the outer ring of new ahmedabad. Everything is far from there.
The Hotel looks dazzling like a gold in the night.

The best and most luxurious hotel in Ahmedabad
Everything is perfect about this hotel. From the time of check in to the luxurious rooms to the room service and the housekeeping.The food is delicious especially the breakfast.
Check out is smooth.
The GM of this hotel Mr Aveekn Sengupra has wonderfully managed this hotel.

The staff hired for catering for VIP section during the finals of ICC WORLDCUP were PATHETIC!!!

Extremely rude, they were missing for hours. They were found in the crowd waiting to take pics of PM Modi at 5:30pm when security preparations started and PM came around 9pm.

The supervisor was going mad trying to control the staff. The staff was back answering to the supervisor and guests.

Since the staff was bad and missing the food was handled very poorly.

For example.
I asked for a bowl for my dal. The server on the other side of the table, doing nothing as guests were filling their own plate, is pointing and showing me where the bowl is. It was right next to him, he was supposed to bring it to me.

Please be careful who you give your company tshirt to. All of them were wearing Taj Skyline tshirts.

Wonderful service and hospitality. Caring staff. Anniversary celebration & decorations made me emotional.
Scope to improve food quality and taste. Clarity of food content should be mentioned in menu. Very inadequate quantity at this rate.

Hotel Taj Skyline is synonymous with excellence. The combination of beautiful rooms, impeccable cleanliness, delectable cuisine at Shamiana, and the helpful and courteous staff makes it the perfect choice for any traveler looking for a truly exceptional experience. I highly recommend this hotel to anyone seeking luxury, comfort, and a memorable stay.

Breakfast was brilliant, Chef Neeraj and his team excelled in all departments, the dinner though was a complete disappointment, no good veg options, very poor staff attentiveness, and overall not upto Tak standards

If you're looking for a luxurious and comfortable stay in Ahmedabad, look no further than the Taj Skyline hotel. From the moment you step foot into the lobby, you'll be blown away by the stunning architecture and elegant design.

The rooms are spacious and beautifully appointed, with all the amenities you could possibly need for a relaxing stay. The bed is comfortable and inviting, and the bathroom is well-stocked with fluffy towels and high-quality toiletries.
The buffet prepared by the Chef Neeraj was excellent and was extremely hospitable. The entire team takes good care of their guests.

Last month I stayed at Taj Skyline and my experience has been great. From check in till check out everything was just upto the mark. Room was excellent as I wanted. Food is mouth watering and service staff has always smile on their face and very polite. Overall a great stay.
Had a great stay - particularly the food quality and variety - catering to varied tastes, for both breakfast and dinner. Kudos to Chef Niraj and team!

Inexperienced staff lack of communication!
Check in took 40 minutes on a prepaid booking we had a 11 month old baby with us!
Construction work was going on in the hotel till 11 pm
had to place 2 phone calls...the night duty manager was rude on the phone...second time was forced to be stern
Room service was excellent
chef was excellent
Not the TAJ experience in any way unfortunately 😕
Hope they improve and train their staff better
I had visited taj multiple times in ahmedabad but the property is value for money 👌👌💯

We stayed at Taj Skyline for 2 nights the service is not good, while check-in the room was not properly cleaned used toiletries were there so for cleaning I have to call reception 3 times.
We booked the dinner buffet at the Taj restaurant itself the food experience was also not good there were hairs in veg rice and they served burned Naan 2 times no one brother about your concerns we paid 4800rs for 2 people's dinner.

Overall experience is not good Try ITC Narmada it's better in this location.

Thank you
Services and food were good, would like to come again.
One of the Finest experience you will have in Ahmedabad. Everything spells CLASS. Food is awesome. Rooms spacious. Fabulous Gym and a pool. On top of that the cooperative and friendly staff. Amen !

I just visited the restaurant area for dinner and found less variety of food as compared to the normal hotel. Yes, no doubt the sweet has much variety to try. However, the main course needs some improvement.

It's Taj.. So naturally it has to be the best and it is. Just one small example of their unparalleled hospitality.. I checked in at 1 am in night and check out at 5.30 am early morning, but at the time of check out they hand over me my packed breakfast bag with enough stuff. Can you believe this? This service we can not expect even at home but this is Taj. They might have prepared this during my short stay at night which is a big thing. Thanks all these people behind this legacy

I recently had the pleasure of dining at Taj Skyline (shamiana), and I must say, it was an exceptional culinary experience that left me absolutely delighted. From the moment I walked into the elegant restaurant, I knew I was in for a treat.
The taste, cuisine, ambience was amazing. Kuddos to Mr neeraj and his team for an wonderful dining experience.
Had a Brilliant Stay Here. Attention to detail and service was top notch.

We stayed at Taj Skyline for 3 nights from 19th August 2023. We had traveled from Kenya for a medical treatment for my husband. On reaching the place, the manager at the desk asked us to wait at the lobby as we were early for check-in. On asking how long we need to wait, we were told we will pay extra charges for early check-in, which we had no issues with! We waited at the lobby for 2 and half hours before being given a room. In this 2and half hour wait, no one offered us any refreshments or even water! Exhausted from the journey, it really was frustrating. During check in time my credit card was swiped and a INR. 10,000 amount was held as a security deposit, however on checking out my card was swiped again by them to pay for all the extras. On questioning them, the boy at the counter said their systems are down and the 10,000rsp will be refunded.
We got a room that was the last of the corridor and we could not rest well, as there was constant construction noise and drilling sounds till late at night.
When we were due for the surgery at the hospital, we left our bags with them at their cloak rooms thinking after discharge we would come back and stay with them for the remaining of the time. But during discharge their rates became too high and we found another hotel. I went back to the hotel to collect my bags and when I asked a boy at the counter I was very rudely asked to wait at the lobby. I waited for 10mins until finally I asked the lady at the entrance to help me locate my bags. I was taken to the cloak room, my bags were removed and I went back to counter to check on the INR 10000 refund or the process of the security deposit. Once again no one really bothered even greeting me!! So I asked the same girl who helped me with the bags to take my message and ask the front office to call me, as I was doing this the counter got free. I started explaining the boy my case and since it was not important he rudely picked an internal call and started talking to his colleague when I was still talking to him explaining my case! I got very frustrated and upset with this behavior!! It was my first ever experience at a Taj property and it was a really bad one! The front office staff need a lot of training and basic etiquette on how to treat guests!
Food was ok, wait staff was not friendly. Once my plate was taken even before I was done eating.
I would not recommend this hotel to any of my friends/family.
I also wish that I get a better response to my complains rather than ‘our sincere apologies and we will work on this’ !!!
Truely a 5 Star✨, Rooms were equipeed with almost everyring oje needs during the stay. The staff is very attentive.

"I had a great stay at this hotel! The service was excellent, and the staff was very friendly. The room was clean and comfortable. The only minor issue was a slight delay in check-in & some services, but it was quickly resolved. Overall, a wonderful experience!"
Hotel super I enjoyed this holiday thank you 😊

Went for a Conference.  Conference hall is on the mezzanine floor, but with a direct driveway ! 👍. Hall for conference/ Event  is large and spacious . Parking is by Valet . Staff was courtesy and polite.  Nice place.

Excellent hotel. I celebrated my birthday and they gave me a complimentary Cake and a reserved tabe with a candle light dinner at their restaurant as I had opted for 1 night stay with Breakfast and Dinner. the staff was so polite and they
I loved the stay. It's was an authentic experience.

Before I get into the details just to say I stay in a few Taj hotels across in India and they are my favourite brand so hold them to high standards.

Now Skyline is a new property and still does not have the Tajness to it. The reception staff are super polite but they are short staff considering Gujarat is a long conversation market. So you end up waiting. Welcome drinks are offered on whim and fancy their is no standard operating procedure.
Rooms are big well equipped. When I checked in I wasn’t informed that there is construction going on and for next few days entire day I had to deal with construction noise. For long stay guests this needs to looked after.
The food is great both breakfast and in room dining.
The welcome room snack is a brilliant intro to the local cuisine. The 2nd floor means washroom basins were always covered with pan spit very disgusting to see and not expected from Taj.
Tiles falling of the entrance and broken on the floor again made the hotel look shabby instead of a Taj.
On a Saturday we got a complimentary high tea which was a great gesture.
So some real positives and some bad misses. I am hoping they will rectify all of this and it will be a top notch choice in the city.

We visited the property in the month of September for our family trip and to celebrate a milestone birthday! The entire staff made our stay really special and memorable. There were a couple of staff members who stood out and made our stay really memorable. He went above and beyond to take really good care of us. F&B staff also took really good care of us .

Nice place, with well appointed rooms and good location. Could be better 1) Lobby has too much of room freshener and it gave me instant headache 2) Shamiana breakfast could be better managed by properly arranging the food and improve order delivery time. It took almost 25 minutes to get my omelette and chai.

Expectations for beautiful were very high
But it was normal.

Yes reception,entrance,staff,security,food,
everything was nice intact very nice.

But rooms OK nothing like wow.

In short, the most HORRIBLE Taj experience ever.

Check in experience at the front desk was very haphazard, unfriendly and harrowing. It was probably the worst checkin we have experienced at any Taj.

At the front desk, I was told about a hold on my credit card for an amount greater than the reservation.  When I asked him to hold the exact amount, the person went ahead and swiped the card. He then told me to pay directly for any extra charges. I have not seen this happen before.

Upon enquiring about an extra bed for each of our rooms, which we paid for during the reservation, we were told that it would not be possible to set beds up since we did not have a triple reservation! This threw us off completely!  Instead of helping, he kept on arguing and then said he would talk to his seniors and revert soon. There was no revert and no extra bed arranged till 9:30pm that night after I had to escalate the matter.

During reservation we had also left a note about our rooms being connected or at least on the same floor. This helps us spend time with our family as much as possible. However, at the front desk we were told that there are no connected rooms. Instead we were offered rooms far apart. Upon insisting we were given rooms across each other on the 8th floor, 816 and 817.

When we entered room 817, we were sad to see the view of the slums. We were not given any kind of heads up.

While taking a shower in the room, water was not draining. The soapy water flooded up within a minute and it was a disgusting experience. Upon calling the front desk they sent a plumber to unclog the drain which was probably not cleaned for a long time.

We wanted to do some sightseeing and have Gujarati thali for lunch and needed some help and guidance from the concierge. When we called the front desk we were told that concierge would call back soon. We waited for 10-15 minutes and did not receive any call back.

After getting fed up with these operational issues I wanted to talk to the GM. He happened to be out of town and I was connected to Mr. Singh, head of operations.

I requested for a refund as this was not the Taj level of service which we have experienced in other locations. After going back and forth with him the entire day I was tired and gave up.

Even the TV had issues. It was powering on but there was no content playing. We tried changing the HDMI input using the remote but that did not help. Upon calling the front desk they sent someone to fix the setup.

There was also construction noise on the 10th floor ceiling with constant hammering starting from 9 am.

Overall a harrowing experience to say the least.

Hopefully, this review will help you have a better experience and Taj to improve their services and truly live up to their claim. We or other guests do not have to go through the harrowing experience with Taj as we did.

We had booked Taj for celebrating the joy of Taj being ranked as India’s Strongest Brand for the third time.

It is pertinent to mention that GM reached out for feedback after our stay. Upon providing the details he provided apologies for the inconvenience we faced and that they are making amends to ensure that future stays are comfortable.

My family and I will think twice before giving Taj Skyline another chance.

It is rather sad to say but this has been the worst Taj experience so far in my life.

It seemed more like a namesake Taj property with substandard services.

Checkin desk was the worse of all, the checkin desk people had zero warmth while welcoming the guest it seemed more like they are forced to give me a room coz i paid for it , and not just me others were also treated the same way.  No details were given about breakfast time, area etc. Just handed the keys and told to go. Which floor to go to, the way to the elevator nothing at all was told to me.

The valet service is even horrible.

Spa service was also pathetic, zero warmth, they behaved as if i was a burden to have booked a massage . The spa therapist was also awful.

The restaurant service was the only good part of the entire hotel, Chef Nitin and Mr. Bharat ensured i felt welcomed and took care of all my requirements.

Apart from the restaurant nothing at all was upto Taj standard, definitely never going to visit here again.

It was a part of Corporate diwali meet.
Very attractive decoration and arrangements made for buffet dinner in hotel lawns. Best in class Quality. Every item in menu selected was best except soup of spinach though best in quality could not lure my taste buds.
So choose your menu items wisely.

Amazing Experience!!

Staying at the Taj Skyline in Ahmedabad was an amazing experience. The host, Ms. Sakshi, was attentive and ensured that we had a comfortable stay. The room was impeccably clean, and the laundry service was speedy. All the room amenities were provided and available when needed. Special thanks for also accomadting my late checkout. The only issue we encountered was when trying to call in-room dining - the phone was not answered for at least 20 minutes, so there is certainly room for improvement. All in all, I had an enjoyable stay and would definitely recommend the Taj Skyline.

I had an exceptional experience at Taj Skyline Hotel! The service provided by the staff was need and clean and the rooms were luxurious and comfortable. And also the hotel's location was also ideal, with easy access to nearby attractions and amenities.

Overall experience was good. Ambience takes the credit. Food not so much. Hospitality needs training. Taj is busy but they need to learn giving care and attention to every client, ignoring client and let them wait not expected from Taj hosts. Shamiana food was average to below average. If you are looking to stay Taj will not disappoint but if family dining and tasty food is your purpose better try somewhere else.

One of the best place to stay in Ahmedabad. It is located at one of the prime locations i.e. Sindhu Bhavan Road. Located on the roadside. The staff is very friendly and warming. The interiors are obviously good. The waiting area is quite accommodating. The staff at the reception is very helful and cooperative. The rooms are very spacious and timely cleaned. The bathrooms are good not that big but adjustable. The service is very timely and good. The buffet is okay and food is not upto Indian taste. You can order ala carte. It was a good experience staying 👍

Rooms are spacious but again the same issue they are lacking in hospitality. we had ordered fresh lime soda but a road side restaurant can get us much better product.

why is Taj staff are not concerned of the basic thing that's customer service. None of the staff great the guest.

sad will never ever stay with any Taj property.

We were here for the brunch with family.. And khow suey soup has won our heart and chef (niraj jha) has personally explained the ingredients in it and the taste was awesome.. Now my mother is diet conscious and she is going to have that for her dinner.. A perfect blend of vegetables and taste.. ❤️

Had an amazing stay here, everything was best.
Ambience of rooms was very nice, calm, and clean.
Had ordered dinner in room, bestest dal makhani we ever had and margarita pizza was yumm too!
Didn’t felt Taj vibe .. food was just ok

As said, Taj and Tata lives upto the expectations.
No words are enough to praise IHCL and their team.

One can look for good times weather it's for business , leisure or family time and celebrations. I strongly believe Taj team do their best to make you feel the warmth of their hospitality.

If to be named : I would like to appreciate Ms. Dhvani and Mr. Kinmat Singh from guest servi e associate at front office , who welcomes you with the generous smile that can make your day.

The moment you enter the property you tend to forget your tiredness and makes you feel fresh.

Mr. Pushpender , Mr. Shubham , and few other from the Restaurant team takes care of all things you need.

The Parantha chef, I forgot the name is too good. On my request they made amazing paneer cheese parantha which was finger picking good.
Food quality is awesome.

Taj service together with Gujrat ambience is best to make you feel awesome.

I would definitely like to revisit again on my next trip.

The property is awesome. Rooms are good and comfortable.
Service is hopeless. Housekeeping staff are good. Spa staff are very good. Problem lies with other staff and management.
As i entered, the girl at the entrance at the lobby was busy in her phone. A thali with dia etc lay near her, but i guess thats only for show, as I dint get any welcome.
The reception guy handed me my room card. NO one accompanied me to the room, I had to go alone, and ask for directions to the lift.
The most idiotic staff are at the morning buffet breakfast. First they won't let you in, and keep you waiting outside the restro with the pretext of no vacant table. But as you span your eyes, you can see 20 percent or more seats empty. On asking, they wud say table is not set. I mean no other hotel does this sort of disservice. It is their inefficiency that they cannot set a table despite so many vacant seats. The main culprit is the manager at the morning buffet, the staff are no better, mostly locals.
The worst of all is the Liquor shop, which is manned by Moronic individuals with zero etiquette. The guy told me, "There is no solution, aapka permit nahi ban sakta".
Finally I made my permit with the help from a good staff and took liquor infront of his face.
Most hillarious part is the General manager apologised to me and on calling the Duty manager, he starts arguing on the liquor issue. If you cannot cooperate and help your guests, it is most deplorable for any hospitality industry, lest alone Taj. Ratan Tata ji must take cognizance of the nuisance these ppl have created and diminishing the brand value of Taj. My worst Taj experience till date.

One of the newer properties. Modern and chic vibes.
Some of the floors are still undergoing some work, so the corridors emanate a not-so-good smell.
Hospitality and food is at par.
It was a great experience! The Chinese cuisine at Nonya is awesome here.  We orderered Chonqing, Hunan style and sea bass. All delicious. Even the watermelon mojito (not in the menu was awesome!

Good hospitality. As is normal for you to expect of any Taj.. warm and welcoming staff. Well maintained rooms. Reasonable rates.
Though a little far from the city.
The breakfast served is delicious and awesome. It is like eat till you drop. Majority items are vegetation which is good thing.

I must Appreciate this property, also cleanliness, but restaurant staff is not up to the mark, morning Breakfast timings at restaurant long waiting period, after long waiting restaurant staff asked to ordered food fast, Breakfast will close shortly. Will only give negative points to restaurant management and breakfast. Or else house keeping staff very perfect and well knowledgeable. As usual Taj is one of my favourite property, but will give 3 star for Taj skyline Ahmedabad. Hope for the best. Otherwise all good. ✌🏻👍🏻
Excellent room service and food. Located in a decent place area. Taj definitely maintain its reputation

We had gone to Shamyana to experience the TAJ hospitality but the a la carte had very little food options, the digital menu was not mobile compatible, the fonts were too small to be readable by my parents and way too expensive compared to other fine dining restaurants.

The accessibility was so easy, the staff was super friendly, the rooms were so clean and luxurious, also they arranged the dinner on my request after the meeting. Overall I highly recommend this place for both vacation or buisness trips.

Food is too Good at the Lounge.
Overall, Taj Skyline in Ahmedabad is a top-notch hotel that offers excellent facilities, impeccable service, and a comfortable stay for both business and leisure travelers

Editing this review a little late. I stated here last year August with my family during a short trip to India.  Best hotel in Ahmedabad . Beautiful rooms, absolutely spectacular breakfast and amazing service.  I had forgotten my passport in the lobby because of the late hour of my checkin. The receptionist took care of it and had it delivered to my room the next day. The beds were super comfy and the breakfast was the best ive had anywhere in the world. Everything from hot Indian savory breakfast to garam garam parathas to Western cuisine.  I have stayed at a lot of resorts world wide and it is safe to say that the Taj Skyline had the best buffet breakfast I've ever experienced.  The rooms themselves are specious and the service phenomenal.  1000% would stay again.

Taj is a magnificent hotel. Food is awesome. Service was excellent. The inside shops are having great stuff. Executive lounge is very nice, they deeply take care of the kind of meal you want to have. Rooms are spacious. Though there was no hair dryer in bathroom. But everything was great. Gym was fantastic.

An magnificent hotel that offers its guests a restful and delightful stay is Ahmedabad's Taj Skyline. The hotel has spacious, well decorated rooms with modern amenities including central air conditioning, televisions with plasma screens, and wireless internet access. I recently had the immense pleasure of staying at Vivanta, Ahmedabad, a five-star oasis of luxury and warmth that exceeded all my expectations. From the moment I stepped into the lobby until the time I reluctantly checked out, every aspect of my stay was truly exceptional.

Upon arrival, I was greeted by the most welcoming and attentive staff I've ever encountered. Their genuine smiles and eagerness to assist immediately made me feel like an honored guest. The check-in process was seamless, and I was escorted to my room, where I was left in awe of the opulent decor and impeccable cleanliness.

The room itself was a haven of comfort and sophistication. The attention to detail in the furnishings and amenities was remarkable. The plush bedding, tasteful decor, and state-of-the-art technology combined to create a space that was both inviting and functional. I particularly appreciated the stunning view from my room, which added an extra layer of enchantment to my stay.

Throughout my visit, the hotel's staff continued to shine. They were always on hand to offer assistance, provide recommendations, or simply engage in friendly conversation. It's evident that the entire team takes great pride in ensuring guests feel not just accommodated, but genuinely cared for.

In summary, my stay at  Vivanta, Ahmedabad was nothing short of exceptional. The combination of opulence and warmth, delivered by the outstanding staff, made me feel like royalty. It's rare to find a five-star hotel that not only exceeds in luxury but also excels in making guests feel genuinely welcome and cherished.

I wholeheartedly recommend  Vivanta, Ahmedabad to anyone seeking an unforgettable and heartwarming five-star hotel experience. I can't wait for the opportunity to return and relive the magic of this extraordinary establishment. Thank you,  Vivanta, Ahmedabad , for making my stay a truly memorable one!

Title: A Midnight Culinary Extravaganza!

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

I recently had the pleasure of experiencing the midnight buffet at Vivanta Ahmedabad, and I must say, it was nothing short of extraordinary! The moment I stepped into the restaurant, I was greeted by a warm and inviting ambiance that set the perfect tone for the evening.

The selection of dishes was simply mind-blowing. From sumptuous Indian delicacies to global cuisines, every dish was a culinary masterpiece. The chefs at Vivanta have truly outdone themselves, showcasing their expertise and creativity. The flavors were exquisite, and the presentation was impeccable.

The live cooking stations added an interactive and engaging element to the dining experience. It was fascinating to watch the skilled chefs in action, and the aromas wafting from the grills were simply irresistible.

What truly impressed me was the attention to detail. The staff were attentive, courteous, and went out of their way to ensure that every guest felt special. The seamless service made the entire evening a delight.

The dessert spread was a sweet symphony of flavors and textures. From decadent cakes to delicate pastries, it was a dessert lover's paradise. Each bite was a heavenly experience.

To top it off, the ambiance was enchanting. The dim lighting, soothing music, and comfortable seating created an atmosphere that was both intimate and relaxing. It was the perfect setting to savor the delectable offerings.

In summary, the midnight buffet at Vivanta Ahmedabad is an absolute must-try for anyone seeking a gastronomic adventure. Whether you're a food enthusiast or simply looking for a memorable dining experience, this is the place to be. My taste buds are still dancing with delight, and I can't wait to return for another culinary journey at Vivanta!

We had arranged a birthday celebration at Vivanta, Ahmedabad for our 1 year old baby.

We were promised that, Vivanta will do some decoration of balloons and flowers for the occassion since we were regular customers over there and quoated accordingly.

When we reached there, we found out that they could not do decoration at all but put a small and literally one bunch of flowers on the dinning table. There was no prior contact to update us or to inform us that why they can't do decoration as promised.

We had other choices too in mind and explored places in Ahmedabad for this occasion.  Also, we believed the brand, promises and past experiences when we went ahead but it was shock and a let down experience. We wanted a place to celebrate and not just to dine. Infact, we were there on last Saturday just to dine.

The overall service could have been better. Some of the item like Soup and bread/roti served cold.

Good part:
• As promised in the quoated price, they did arrange cake which was lovely.

Down points:
• No decoration as promised/ no contact or an update that they could not do it.
• Food was good but some items served cold.
• Need to increase overall staff capacity for better service and customer attention.
• The cake was fresh but they took a lot of time in preparation, the function and lunch should have started at 13:00 with celebration first. The cake cut was done around 14:00ish.

In brief, the guest felt like dine out and not some celebration. We as customer could not do much at last minute hence overall big let down for the birthday occasion and will not consider Vivanta as venue for any such celebrations in future.

Regards
Kunal Patel

Only problem is during rush days ( so called black out dates ), all of the charges like food, room tariff and facilities are hyped by whims. The sales team is quite difficult in communicating the negotiation deals. Better to have quality food from outside. The pool area is well decorated. There is a basic gym available at the hotel. The entrance to the gym and pool area is beautified. There are different restaurants for A la Carte and buffet or thali items. Sometimes we faced a communication problem with the staff members working in the restaurants. Also, there is a problem to commute by the lift as the card may not work properly while going to floors other than one's own or the ground which seemed inconvenient many s times. *Disappointing Experience at Courtyard by Marriott*

Rating: 1/5

I recently had the misfortune of staying at the Courtyard by Marriott, and my experience was marred by several issues.

*Unacceptable Minibar Policy*

Upon checking out, I was surprised to find additional charges for minibar consumption, which was not communicated to me at check-in. This lack of transparency is unacceptable, especially when guests are not explicitly informed about the charges.

*Rude Reception Staff*

To make matters worse, the reception staff were unapologetic and dismissive when I brought this to their attention. Their attitude was unprofessional and unwelcoming, leaving a sour taste.

*Subpar Food Quality*

The hotel's restaurant served mediocre food, lacking flavor and freshness. For a Marriott property, I expected better culinary standards

*Rating Breakdown*

- Service: 1/5
- Food: 2/5
- Value: 1/5
- Overall: 1/5

*Suggestion to Management*

1. Clearly communicate minibar charges to guests at check-in.
2. Train reception staff to be more empathetic and professional.
3. Improve food quality and variety.

I hope the hotel takes these concerns seriously and makes necessary improvements.

"Hospitality to Hospital!"

While this hotel may be one of the most well-known names in Ahmedabad, my experience left much to be desired. Imagine this: an early morning meeting at 7:45 AM, most of us already battling the Navratri-induced sleep deficit, eagerly awaiting a fresh cup of coffee to kickstart the day... only to find absolutely nothing but stale tea, looks like the hospitality industry is trying to generate more business for hospitals by serving stale tea. Yes, you heard that right—no coffee, no cookies, and the tea tasted like someone had added garam masala instead of tea masala. To add to the charm, there was just one confused staff member in sight to assist us.

For a hotel of this caliber, one would think basic morning refreshments wouldn't be too much to ask. But alas, if you're planning to visit, bring your own caffeine survival kit. You’ll thank me later!

Disappointing Experience at Courtyard Marriott Banquet Hall, Ahmedabad

My recent experience at the Courtyard Marriott banquet hall in Ahmedabad left a lot to be desired. While the venue boasts a spacious capacity for 800 guests, several shortcomings fell short of the Marriott standard.

Spacious but Lacking in Signage

The hall itself can definitely accommodate a large crowd, which is a plus. However, navigating to the specific hall within the complex proved to be a challenge. Signage seems lacking, making it difficult to find the designated function room.

Food Fit for Snacking

The food, while delicious, felt more like upscale finger foods or hors d'oeuvres rather than a full-fledged banquet meal. This might not be ideal for all types of events.

Accessibility Issues

A major drawback was the lack of a lift or elevator directly accessible from the underground parking area. This forces guests to walk all the way up to the main entrance, which can be inconvenient for those with mobility limitations or during extreme weather conditions.

Unkempt Washrooms

The cleanliness of the washrooms was another disappointment. Unkempt restrooms do a disservice to the overall experience, especially considering the reputation of the Marriott brand.

Overall, my experience at the Courtyard Marriott banquet hall fell short of expectations. While the spaciousness is a positive, the lack of clear signage, limited food options, accessibility concerns, and unclean restrooms bring the overall rating down significantly. It's important to note that this review reflects my personal experience, and yours may differ.

Courtyard by Marriott: Good Bones, But Wait for the Remodel
The Courtyard by Marriott has a lot of potential, but unfortunately, the ongoing renovations are impacting the overall experience. The staff is a definite highlight - friendly, helpful, and eager to please. Breakfast is also a winner, with a tasty spread and live stations for paratha and eggs. The rooms themselves are comfortable and a decent size.
However, the renovations are a major drawback. The spa and salon are closed, which is a bummer for anyone looking to relax and pamper themselves during their stay. Construction noise and disruptions are also likely to be an issue.
Overall, the Courtyard by Marriott is a good hotel with comfortable rooms and a great breakfast. However, I would recommend waiting until the renovations are complete before booking a stay.

A Nightmare Dining Experience at Marriott

I am rarely so moved to pen a review as I was after my horrendous meal at Marriott. Everything from the service to the food was well below par.

My order of paneer tikka, usually a delight of Indian cuisine, arrived at my table aggressively spicy, almost a culinary assault. It felt less like a dish to be savored and more like a challenge from a chef with something to prove. Unfortunately, the only thing proved was their lack of skill in balancing flavors.

The risotto, typically a symphony of creamy goodness, was utterly tasteless. It was as if the chef had forgotten that this Italian classic relies heavily on its delicate flavor profile. It was a travesty to all risotto everywhere, a bland and uninspired dish.

Adding insult to injury, the waiter outright refused to replace our meals. This, coupled with an abysmal level of service that ranged from uninterested to outright rude, completed the trifecta of dining horrors. I was left in complete disbelief of how a restaurant under the Marriott banner could let its standards slip this far.

In conclusion, I would caution anyone against dining at this establishment. With its poor quality of food, horrific service, and a general disdain for its patrons, the Marriott has deeply tarnished its reputation. A once reliable name in dining has sadly fallen from grace. Do yourself a favor and avoid this place like the plague. You deserve better.
