Excellent place to stay in Ahmedabad. In the heart of the city, this beautifull sturcture is well maintained and wonderfully decorated. Ever smiling and helpful staff adds grace to your stay.

Rooms are amazingly designed and are very very spacious. You will find a few books kept in your room and the lobby area too. Rooms are not cheap but definately value for money.

Staff extensively used local language and Hindi though they could speak English too. Gives a cosy and homely feeling.

I was disappointed by th food provided at the restaurant called The Green House. It is definitely an over rated place.

The other restaurant - Agashiye is very costly as Thalis start at minimum of Rs. 999+ taxes.

If I am visiting Ahmedabad again, I will always prefer to go back here.

Thanks House of MG for your hospitality. I have recommended you to a few people already :)
Attended wedding here. Superb location. Food was amazing and hospitality was gr8

This was one of my best experiences in any place of stay.
The  decor is homely and luxurious. Attention to detail is fantastic. Rooms are vary spacious and well designed for use.
The famous Thali if not the best is well recommended. A full rich experience of Gujarati cuisine.

However the reason why this property is above all o have experienced so far is the attitude of the members who take care of the guests. I do not call them staff and their action service because each and everyone takes care as if they are the host.. very respectful. Very accommodating.
I happened to meet the lady GM taking a round of the property and she too was extremely warm in her behaviour.

I would definitely come back to this place.

If you want to pick one place in Ahmedabad to stay.. choose this. You won't regret it ever.

Beautiful building with great history. Had amazing experience here. The dinner was on terrace which had the whole view of ahmedabad roads. Looked more beautiful at night. The food was fantastic and authentic Gujarati cuisine. A must visit.

This place is very amazing and sits right in the center of the city. The heritage is preserved very well with exceptional food. The prices are on a little high side compared to local pricing. Just across sidi saiyad jali, and major market makes the surrounding exceptionally live. They also organize a heritage walk. This is a must visit place to explore culture and local cuisine at once

Very interesting hotel in the historic house.  Like you are staying in the museum! Nice, but cold pool. Very good spa and massages! Only breakfast is poor - it’s only veg omelettes. But staff is very helpful and polite, I’m very appreciate for their assistance.

Undoubtedly, one of the best restaurants of Ahmedabad and in my top 5 list. This is definitely an upscale place right in the heart of the city; so expect it to be pricey. The moment you enter this place, it is difficult to believe that it is situated in the noisy old Ahmedabad as the it calms you down with it's ambience. It mostly serves Gujarati items - ala carte on ground floor and unlimited on the first floor. While there is a long list scrumptious Gujju dishes to choose from, you can't miss their jalebis!  Do not miss to give a dekko to their beautiful rooms and swimming pool after you are done with you dinner/lunch.
Poor food quality, after charging 2056 rupees per person, all food was stale and very poorly managed services, the taste is too spicy and nothing is fresh at all, never go to house of MG

Love the way they have managed to maintain the ambience. So raw! Love the mist fans, the greenery! Love the way the staff dresses: plain white kurta pajama! Love the simplicity, the variety in menu; and each dish so authentic ! I drool over Malpua, Upma, Ras-Poori ... And what not ! Definitely a go-to place in a time where all the cafés nearly have similar feeling!

Yesterday i.e on 8th October, 2023 Sunday evening, we seven friends had a dinner at 'Agashiye" Restaurant  at House of MG. The dinner was very delicious and all the food menu was very nice. The ambience was very cosy and comfortable. Service right from the reception counter Ms Maria to the dinner staff all were very courteous and polite. This is a must visit restaurant. We are one of the loyal and regular guests at Agashiye. Regards CA Vishal Dave

Traditional gujrati thali is best here.. unlimited food..good staff..very good ambience here and road view is there. Food here is prepared by them , even ice creams are their hand made. Pure food and a very good experience. Plates here are of brass or silver depending upon your preference and their hospitality is too very good. It was a very good experience there.

Got married here so I’ll always have a soft spot for this place. Rightly so, the food is delectable and the experience is incredible. It’s Gujarati food taken a notch higher with good ingredients and quality service

Amazing place to stay, very cozy place to stay in heritage city. This place is a cultural epitome of the city. Every about this place is simply wow. Pool is not that great, breakfast is OK ok.

Amazing Gujarati Thali quite expensive but worth the money, you need to go hungry to enjoy. Best was Brinjal subzi, I had atleast 5 times, which reminded of kathirikaai puzhi kozhumbu.

* Be careful with the switches they are old with no back cover placed next mesh curtains of the bed , I got shock.

That is the reason for 4 star.

There are 2 restaurants. One in ground floor and other on terrace .
The one on terrace serves Thali's and one one the ground floor has a variety of food .
I visited the one on ground floor . service is perfect and real quick . Few dishes served less quantity than routine but very tasty.
Staff are very polite ( one will get that Royal feeling ) ..
Ambience is Supra great .
Do try the jalebi with vanilla .
One can have a look around the heritage hotel . It's splendid the way it's maintained .
Art gallery is good for visual treat , but very costly . Din seem worth it .
Note . Just entering u will notice a pillar which is made of 10 and 5 Paisa coins . Do see . It's really good .
Perfect place to go multiple times .

A old Haveli which had been converted to a restaurant serving traditional Gujarati cuisine. The setting is quite lovely with charming interiors and Gujarati decor. The food is good but only if you have a taste for Gujarati cuisine and are willing to eat lots and lots of variety of dishes.

Although I stayed at one of their sister properties, I had breakfast at The Green House at The House is MG. Breakfast was nice and healthy. Coffee was delicious. Staff was attentive and friendly. I wandered around the hotel a bit to check it out. Such a beautiful place. The history behind the place is quite interesting, too. Their are family photos well worth checking out. Search for the swing - catch a peaceful moment before heading out in Ahmedabad.

The food was amazing here. But a bit overpriced. You can get a similar experience in other restaurants like Rajdhani at half the price.
Service was amazing and well trained staff.
Overall a great experience.

A 5 star experience in a heart of a city ... We grabbed a chance to stay there for a day, otherwise staycation in same city was just not a trend 3 years before COVID.
It is indeed a must must must go place actually for whosoever find antique structure soothing.
Antique beds, room design, a zula in room, in corridor instead of sofas, there were zula... Manmade little waterfalls... indeed Beautiful!

It was a pleasure to dine out in an open terrace with friends with wonderful and authentic Gujarati food. The ambience adds to the feeling of old world charm. Food roots you to Gujarat and on top of it one is made to feel special by the attentive staff out there.

Wonderful Experience..Great Place For Heritage And Culture Lover People. The House of MG is a premier boutique heritage hotel in Ahmedabad, the UNESCO declared World Heritage City. One of the oldest buildings in the city. Here Heritage Is Accepted Modern Technology.

This is one of the best and iconic places in Ahmedabad.  We had the silver Gujarati thaali at Agashiye that cost us about INR 2000 per thaali. This was my second visit. Loved every item on the thaali. My friend from outside Ahmedabad loved it! It's the costliest thaali in Ahmedabad. But worth it. The ambience and service was absolutely welcoming and warm!

Beautifully restored mansion in the older colonial style of the area, with attentive staff and lovely ambience in a very central location. The restaurant upstairs provides a beautiful Gujarati thali which I highly recommend if you want to try the local food. It is only vegetarian.
Grand and luxurious enough. The ambience is unique. The thali though is overpriced. The location is in the heart of the old city hence fraught with parking woes

Most exclusive and uniquely sophisticated restaurant to entertain guest to ahmedabad with local and regionals cuisine. The food is authentic in taste and  truly mouthwatering. The way of presentation and courtesy of staff is remarkable. As it's qualitative, it comes with a price. Costly it is by Ahmadabad standard, however it is worth a visit

I visited the house of MG for a family lunch because my mom was chef for the Marathi thali.  So I won't comment on food otherwise it will be a bias food review.

Other than that, I feel worthless experience. Poor service quality. It was bigger name but poor quality. Not even proper sitting chairs. Very noisy. I will choose to go Taj skyline than going to heritage hotel.

Nice historical place
Less crowded
Grate hospitality
Both restaurant and cafe at their best

Rate was bit higher side and Gujarati people may also found food is same like home.

Food authentic Gujarati even not added other falevor or spices like other restaurants does the same.

Authentic Gujarati cuisine fine dining restaurant in heart of the city.

5 * for ambience
5* for service
4* for price point , reason is its price vs quantity doesn’t matches.
5* for quality of food.

They have terrace restaurant Agashiye , which has thali option with lots of authentic Gujarati cuisine items but per person dining cost is bit higher. Once in a year kind of restaurant for premium fine dining experience.

The house of MG is beautifully made heritage hotel with antique objects placed at every nook and corner.. The interiors are made to match the rich gujarati taste. Green house cafe served good food but lacked hospitality during our meal.. Otherwise it's a good place for a quick supper or dinner.

The property is beautiful and we stayed in the Heritage Suite for 5 days. The room was well maintained and comfortable. The service of the staff however left much to be desired and was not up to the mark. There was no ‘service attitude’ especially when we were trying to book a table at their restaurant or trying to get them to book a car. Before leaving they were trying to get us to give a tip, which I was willing to. One of the house keeping staff said, we had used one of the items from the mini bar (which we never did)

Every request was met with why it can’t be done. Found it quite strange. Parul and her staff at the ala carte restaurant were good.

The place is nice but the staff needs some serious training

I didn't like their service and hospitality too much. Although the property n all is good, the staff is not at cooperative, particularly the people in the restaurant. Be cautious about having late dinner coz as soon clock strikes 1030, even though you are in middle of dinner they might not serve you even extra chapatis. You ask them 2 spoonful of khichadi for baby, they will charge half plate even for that.
The location of property is pretty good. We had grand suite which was pretty spacious and well decorated with artifacts.

Rooms are very neat and clean. It has a great heritage feel. The place has a hundred year old history. This hotel is in the midst of a crowded area. Too noisy, it doesnt have the calm and serenity thats required for a peaceful stay.  The breakfast lacks variety.
Very nice hospitality and delicious food

Amazing old colonial bungalow in the old part of Ahmedabad, that has been restored and preserved. Makes you feel like you are re-living history. The restaurant on the rooftop was incredible! Amazing authentic food which was well worth the money. The locals might find it pricey however if you are a tourist you have to visit this place.

It's a heritage hotel. Mainly concentrated on foreign customers. The stay here is moderate but the food is too much expensive. I had an experience of having Thali One day which they cost 1135/- and 1500/- approximately exclusive of taxes. Another day was having and experience of la carte menu which was serving a simple small bowl of vegetable as the half vegetable costing range from 190-250 per bowl. I felt it too expensive but lucrative and tasty. If one want to enjoy the heritage of India that too Gujrat than one must go and have an experience for atleast two days of stay and both kind of food available.

Grand old place with old world charm serves a lavish Gujarati Thali in metal plates. Great place to have dinner in Agashiye restaurant  i.e. on the terrace. The have openair as well as AC dining. Well worth it if you are visitor to Amdavad. One can get a bird's eye view of the  Sidi Saiyyad Mosque opposite the road.  Did not stay in House of MG so cannot rate the rooms but I am sure it will be worth the experience of a Maharaja.

Food, ambience, service and food (just to emphasize) is top notch. Don't expect a spicy roller coaster, but a gentle introduction to the complex Cuisine from Gujarat. The place is wonder land, a great mix of modernist and heritage elements. Green house, Agashiye and Pol Kholi, everyone of the joint is worth exploring.

Extremely friendly staff and reasonably priced hotel accommodation. Fairly close to most (if not all) tourist attractions. The hotel also has a travel desk that can arrange for hire cars for 4 and 8 hours. If you get a room which is racing the main road, you may be kept awake at night because of the traffic noise from the main road. The restaurant in the hotel serves good food - authentic gujrati as well as multi cuisine dining. The hotel has various quiet corners where you can have a good silent and relaxing time. Overall, very happy with the hotel and its staff!

Wow! Words are less to narrate. I must say that if you have been to Ahmedabad and haven't been to HMG, you have really missed something. Narrates the glorious past. Agashiye is too good.and people around are more than very good. Loved it!

The House of MG - Name itself replicates alot about the hotel. I love the theme of hotel based on traditional House. Interior and extrior of hotel is based on that like a big haveli in Pol(Gujarati Name of a Society). Infact there used to reside a big joint family of businessman many years ago. I like the uniform of staff very much gives me the touch of True of Indian Culture unless like other star hotels where the uniform is formal attire. Everything in the hotel i liked it very much. Swimming pool design is also wonderful and attractive. Food quality is great and taste is very delicious. One of the best hotel i have ever reside until now. Few exceptions are accecptable. One of the best thing i like for the food is that considering the hotel size they are serving vegetarian menu and was very happy to see tourists from other nations are staying there happily with no complaints.

This is an over hyped place. They mainly want to attract foreign tourists who do not mind paying anything in the name of Heritage property. The property is very old and somehow being maintained at 3 star category hotel level. The facilities are negligible and even for car drop to airport which is part of the agreed hotel tariff, one needs to haggle and argue. Finally they relent and make guest share a shuttle for airport drop. I have never experienced this kind of hospitality in any hotel. They have over priced everything, including the F&B rates. The food is good,but does not justify the premium being charged. Will recommend to not get enthralled by rosy reviews. They have marketed themselves really well, that's it.

Overall the restaurant is good with good ambiance. The thali taste was good overall but out of the 4 sabji in the menu baigan bhartha was the only exceptional as it waa cooked with desi style with full of smoky taste. Rest items like Rabdi, farsan etc was okay okay for me. They make items keeping in mind international visitors that could be one of the reason that I being native Gujarati felt rest items okay okay. Overall very over priced and you will feel its not value for money.

One of the wonderful experience. Excellent ambience. The traditional taste of Gujarati food. Variety of items. Excellent quality of food. Warm welcome at the entrance of restaurant. Very Cooperative staff. One must visit with family or friends. Very nice view from the agashiye restaurant. Please do like my review if you find it helpful. Thank you very much.

Beautiful,well maintained premises.Very helpful& friendly staff.The only concern was mosquitoes,a lot of them & mosquito repellent which was provided did not work at all.We brought it to their notice& they provided another mosquito repellent but even this was not working on mosquitoes.Had a difficult night & mosquito bites.In the room seating for 2 people is inadequate,had to make makeshift arrangement ourselves.I have deducted 2 stars for that.
Agashiye thali was just ok in terms of flavours as compared to other places in amdavad,so would call it expensive..though the ambience & the experience itself was very good.

Visited this place for a family lunch on a Saturday. Classic example of how poor service can overshadow exceptional quality food. Long delays, unprofessional suggestions and novicely unthoughtful service turned down the entire experience.  Classy ambience and smiling  staff are the plus points.

It has been always a great experience at MG house but this time we went on Friday lunch & got not Very fresh chutneys & farsan which we usually  were offered at our earlier visits. But it yes we did get the best sukhdi there in the dessert which made our day. They have better options on Saturday & Sunday.

Con Job it’s my idea of a perfect chori setting. Only foreigners get fooled by all this.

Rooms while large, are dark and dinghy very little sunlight.

Service levels are appallingly bad.

Everything is way overpriced and portion sizes are extremely small. I am a small eater and the sabji wasn’t enough for me!!!

Breakfast are bad, same menu everyday, dosa was not ground well the rice was still unground and I had to spit it out, upma was like glue could stick envelope with it.

A cup of masala chai costs 155 rupees

Its in a very downmarket part of the city sndnot safe for women tourists.

Best avoided

Amazing ambience and even more amazing authentic Gujarati cuisine. The haveli is beautiful, especially at night. The roof top Agashiye Thali restaurant is the best Gujarati thali you will get. The ground floor restaurant serves delicious snacks and has options for non Gujarati snacks also (though I am sure you should stick to the amazing local cuisine).

One of the best Hotels in the old Ahmedabad city.
And also possibly in new city as well.
Haveli look gives vintage feel but still manages to provide all the modern amenities.
Very nicely laid out interiors.
Food is amazing and must try.
Service is little slow.

Very fine boutique hotel.  The building is absolutely gorgeous.  Very big room with authentic furnitures.
Location is close to the Bazar and the old city but the street is noisy so ask fir a back room. Stuff are kind and helpful.

This is my favourite Hotel! I love the building and the details of the inventive interiors! All the restaurants are delicious and the staff is so very kind and helpful. The bathrooms are great. The pool is out of this world. If you needed anything the senior supervisor of Housekeeping, Mr.Hasmikh is willing and efficient. The new store have the most fabulous books and curated crafts of infinite detail. The exhibition they have mounted is so very special! Compared to other museums in the City, it has wonderful display systems and the curating first class. This is my third stay at the house and each stay has been perfection. Do not miss the experience of staying here or visiting the shop and restaurants!

The food price might be little higher than the usual good places but you must try it when you're in Ahmedabad. There are 3 thali options available and we took Light thali which was 999+GST+service charges+donation. The only difference is that you won't be getting a dessert. But the ambiance of this place is mesmerizing.

Excellent hotel in the middle of a crazy city. When they say it feels like home they don't lie. It really does. Enjoyed our little stay here. Mr shadab was very helpful and warm. That helped us a lot. Will surely come back here in the future.

And we did and it still is as great as the last time. Amazing experience. Very good food. A little oasis in the middle of a mad city

Very neat place. The old times feel is great. It's like being in another era. The staff post-covid scenario seems to cope well, though a lot can still be done. Hygiene is good. Love the wooden old-fashioned ambience. Quiet place for a rewind. Food at Agashia is very good & light too.

The thali we tried out was a Kasa thali at Agashiye that costed for 1400/- for one person. Also loved their Anjeer Icecream and Basundi.

They have offer for early bird and late flyers where you can get 20% off on Thalis. This offer is only available from Monday to Friday.

The cuisine will remind you of a home cooked meal with fresh, seasonal ingredients that are mildly spiced and the hospitality and ambience will make you feel like a privileged guest in a grand private home.

Their restaurant Agashiye is an awesome place to sample Gujarati cuisine. I've become a fan and I can swear by every single item on their menu. Beautiful ambience, warm hospitality and neverending supply of food is definitely an overwhelming experience. A must do for everybody visiting Ahmedabad.

If I could give this hotel 6 stars, or even 10, I would! It is by far the best place I have EVER stayed - the hotel is beautiful, traditionally decorated and absolutely unique in every way, with a lovely pool and lots of common areas to sit and read. The on-site restaurants both serve delicious food and the gift shop is well worth a browse. But the best thing by far about this hotel is the staff - every single person who works at House of MG went above and beyond to make my stay as comfortable and special as possible. Whenever I walked into reception or sat down in the restaurant, I felt like I was meeting friends. They truly made my visit to Ahmedabad.
Nice charm, go to place for us whenever my folks are in town. Hands down the best Gujarati thali ever. The rooms have a nice archaic charm and is a great blend of modern and heritage.

The House of MG, Bhadra Road, Ahmedabad :

I am still in awe of this place, ever since I attended the wedding ceremonies there for two days!

It provides a beautiful levish ambience to celebrate and organise any big event with limited audience.

The hospitality was very warm and welcoming, which pleased all the guests.

The food here was simply awesome, with a good variety of tasty food, be it starters and soups (being served continuously), or the proper meal (lunch and dinner).

The structures within and around give a proper feel of destination wedding, with their look-and-feel (see photos).

Overall, a perfect place for organizing such levish events!!

Beautiful rooms but service was a let down.

ROOMS: 4/5
Being a former palace, the rooms had grand proportions, the bedroom had a swing in it and the bathroom was the size of most bedrooms. There were great touches to hide modern equipment such as wooden boxes made around the light/fan switches and a wooden antique wardrobe built into the wall - it was too small to be of use but a nice touch.

There were no blackout blinds in the bedroom and the curtains were very flimsy, unfortunately our bedroom overlooked the restaurant and busy main road beyond so light and noise constantly came into our room.

LOCATION: 3/5
Set on a busy roundabout on a main road, opposite a well visited mosque meant it was noisy and polluted. There were several restaurants a stone's throw away and getting a rickshaw was easy as there were plenty going past but the short moments spent standing on the "street" negotiating fares etc were pretty tense.

SERVICE & FACILITIES: 2/5
The hotel had a small but functional gym, a ridiculously overpriced restaurant - similar food served across the road at a quarter of the price - and a nice looking pool which was too cold for us to swim in.

Service was a real disappointment, simple requests needed two to three phone calls to check on, for example, it took several hours and numerous reminders before Ethan's bed was finally delivered, meaning a delayed bedtime and all the things that follow on from that.
I did complain about the service and saw a short-lived improvement before normal (bad) service resumed.

The staff were friendly enough, especially the porters and cleaning staff.

VALUE FOR MONEY: 2/5
This was an expensive place to stay, around £125 per night for a grand suite with breakfast. If the service and location matched the standard of the rooms, it would have been money well spent but taking that into consideration, I feel like there are better options available.

A BIT ABOUT US:
We're currently on a four month trip around Asia & the South Pacific with our 3 year old son, if you'd like to read more reviews, search for Ethans Day to find our blog and YouTube channel.

Anyone with a love for old world charm should stay here. They have typically Gujarati delicacies served best. The haveli has a beautiful history too. The staffs need to be well trained, but Pradeep on the front desk was very helpful to us and made sure all the shortcomings were covered up. Over all loved the rooms and corridors with swings all around.

Oh oh oh what to say about this place. Best gujarti food in the town far far better than Swati snack. Just little bit costly but much worst visiting.
Taste of food is really good.
The place it self is so good that you will be in love with that whole heritage building.
Staff is really good.

A remarkable oasis of tranquility and calm peppered with culture and Indian history situated in the heart of Ahmedabad. A truly amazing building carefully abd meticulously restored for you to enjoy. Take a moment to try the food, taste the coffee or simply walk around - you won't be disappointed.
Chef Parul is a rare combination of talent and hospitality. She and her staff went above and beyond to host my mother’s birthday.

We wnt for the dinner at agasiya. One of the place much popular due to house of mg claimed that its heritage place. Infortunately the treatment given not only to us but i observed many others were worst. It starts from reception. Non professionals mannerless person holding place to welcome you!!!. With advance payment and meaning less waiting yoh would be asked to have soup and starter first at one stand by area!!!! Then they will provide actual rable for dinner and stary bombarding stiffs to feed you like u have to dinish in hurry. Absolutely non professional way of serving food. Shame on management. Better not to go as it would spoil our mood.
Food is awesome we had Gujarati thali silver one. Should have a kids menu too as my kid couldn’t eat the food.

Grossly overpriced, strictly not for the Indians as they will not enjoy. Any village theme restaurant will be better unless you want to eat in the silver thalis. This is an experience but at very high price.. this can be good place for foreigners to experience hygienic Indian food.

There are 2 aspects, the heritage place where this restaurant is located is amazing 4.5 stars for that. But service is visibly with an intent to "show" but not serve.

Variety of items offered in Thali are great, but they typically serve rapidly in quick succession and then just disappear or just acknowledge but not serve. While its a typical theme at Thali restaurants, it was distinctly visible and experienced.

located on a 5 star property, in the heart of the city, the theme of the restaurant is really well thought. food was delicious and the people were really friendly and accepting any requests they were handed to them.
a bit more variety for the price could be left wanting for, but overall a decent experience.

Beautiful and elegant. True taste of ahmedabad city's elite from mill owners era. It used to be a personal house of citys very rich family converted into hotel and rooftop restaurant serving fantastic Gujarati food.
Review for Aagashiya roof top restaurant. Good traditional decor. Open to air and indoor seating. Serve delicious Gujarati thali cuisine.

Was here for team Lunch with corporate team. One of the most exotic Thali experience in the country. Thanks to Heritage building, Silver vessels, detailing in serving, curation of the food, huge list of food accompaniments and stupendously crafted desserts.

This place is more of an slow relishing bon voyage in totality rather than just food. Needs time, patience and indeed roughly INR 2000 to beleive :)

This heritage place deserves accolades for their nicely designed menu, dishes with authentic taste and novel green decor. The richness of history with the nice service makes one want to go back again and again.

Beautiful heritage place, best place for shopping aunthentic stuff.
I ve been to the green house, really amazing place with open kitchen, surrounded by greenary, and gorgeously delicious food.
Social distancing is nicely maintained, hygiene level is also maintained carefully which is the need of these hours.
So u guys cherish yr time going to this heritage place, dont forget to visit the museum.

Stay blessed stay happy

The House of MG is awesome!  The staff were great and very friendly.  The Green House Cafe was great and Agashiye was an experience.  I highly recommend staying here if you are going to be in Ahmedabad and especially if you are interested in seeing the old city and its many pols.

The location is easily reachable. Rooms are good, clean and well maintained. The look of rooms is somewhat like 90's. The service is good. Inside hotel there is mini waterfall for decor and an indoor swimming pool    makes it a better place. Overall impression was nice!

The House of MG is what it is popularly called. Situated in Ahmedabad, the property is reckoned to be a boutique heritage hotel and is by far one of the best hotels in Ahmedabad city. The quaint charm, the well-designed rooms, commendable services and state of the art facilities have propelled this hotel one of the most preferred places to stay in Gujarat. Here, one can get acquainted with the rich Gujarati culture and the glorious lifestyle of the erstwhile Maharajas. You are simply going to love this place if luxury is what you have in mind.
Amazing hotels, wonderful staff, wonderful food. Everyone is so nice and helpful. Will definitely be staying next time I am in town.

An exhilarating experience awaits for those who want to have lunch or dinner at Agashiye. From hospitality, ambience, taste of food to range of delicacies, Agashiye offers the most delectable of indulgences. The place is highly recommended for everyone visiting Ahmedabad and looking for a place to be captivated by the flavour of authentic Gujarati dishes. Agashiye will be an unforgettable experience.

Very Nice hotel & Building. Gujarati movie wrong side raju's song Satrangi shooted here.
Amazing place. Here 90℅ foreigners stay and it is famous for its Gujarati Thali
But it's too costly around 1300 per person. And simple Gujarati food

You'll find a flair for traditional living in Gujarat. A perfect blend of ethnic interiors and modern amenities.

Nevertheless service requires a bit of improvement.
Went there with very high expectations but unfortunately the food was much below that. The quantity was shameful and service was pathetic. Drinks were extremely bad! Overall bad experience.

Very pleasant place in the centre of old district in Ahmadabad. They serve good food, but it is the ambience and vibe of this place which makes me revisit this place over and again. Was little disappointed today since they didn't service ala carte breakfast. Buffet was very basic and didn't offer much option.

I appreciate the restoration and keeping the heritage outfit of the property. Good food and  great atmosphere. It's always good to be here again and again. Today I just realised that a bit more attention  if paid in the service part would be great. Keep up the good work

A beautiful vintage hotel with two restaurants and a beautiful shop. The resturant Agashiye serves authentic gujrati thali with good ambiance . "The green house" is the other restaurant . It has a scope for improvement in food , presentation and cutlery. At such a vintage and beautiful place the melamine cutlery looks out of place.
The place has a distinctive old world heritage flavour but with a modern glass elevator. Great place for a thali in world style though some dishes are overly spicy.
Place where you can get authentic gujrathi thali . Experience was Worth the price.

Ahmedabad is divided between the new city and the old one. All the interesting spots of the city are on the old city and in the middle of it all is the house of MG. A beautiful restored villa, the stuff was very nice, the room (and you should upgrade to the best one) is amazing, there are a few restaurants, spa and a beautiful indoor pool. It’s a very good start point to many interesting places. The free night tour is very good and interesting. Also, they arranged me a whole day tour with a guide that was very good

We had dinner at Agashiye, very good experience for a Gujarati Thali. Nice spread, service and ambience.

Overall a bit expensive, but worth it.

Superb hospitality and gorgeous place to stay. The antique feel this exuded was an experience of a kind. 😍
Centrally situated so all the major travel destinations are nearby.
They offer complementary breakfast which was scrumptious.
They have a guided tour options, which cover nearby hawelies, temples, jama masjid, etc. We opted for breakfast tour which starts around 10 (depending on the number of tourists) and lasts for one and half/2 hrs. The guide was very knowledgeable and knew multiple languages. Some of the hawelies had breathtaking work.
Also comfy bed was provided for the 3rd guest. They have in house swimming pool, gym, ethnic store and a popular restaurant 'Agashiye'. So if you are taking an auto you just need to tell them take me to 'Agashiye'. 😁
I strongly recommend this one as the rates are reasonable and the service is top notch.
Just because of this place our trip was much more relaxing and memorable. Thanks to the cordial staff.

The outdoor cafe is perfect to end an old Ahmedabad tour. The food and ambience is lovely. The textile museum and shop have great collections! The entire haveli is beautifully maintained with contemporary elements in an age old structure.

An excellent heritage property right in the middle of Ahmedabad & opposite the world famous 'Sidi Saeyed Ni Jalli', with awesome Gujarati food prepared hygienically. If you want to experience first hand, how the Gujarati Shethias (effluent class) lived, book your room over here.

Authentic local cuisine, excellent customer service. The rooms are brilliantly kept, with excellent tile work and interiors. The room service charge costs a fortune. Lunch/ dinner at Agashiye (one of their in house restaurants) is a must.

What a lovely hotel! Grand rooms, open restaurant and one of the best Gujrati Thali served in Ahmedabad. In the old part of the city, but a short walk from the Heritage parts. A must stay place if on a visit on a vacation.
Great place with amazing food choices n quality!can go with friends as well as family!

Had India been a foreign country, it would have been like this place. Food just like home, unique architecture, no unnecessary loud music, Sidi Saiyad ni jali just in front of you. A majestic experience.

Beautiful heritage hotel, loves to come here again & again.
Agasaye is my favorite restaurant .fully vegetarian,food prepared in Gujarati /Rajasthani style.museum,gift shop worth visiting.

Love to coma again & again.
Good authentic Gujarati food. After 2 PM many of menu items not available. After taking order you won't be served food as its finished. In store except Gujarati items everything is available
Great ambience. However, the experience has the related charges. If you're not worried about the bill, go for the thaali experience in this heritage restaurant.

Got a chance to visit here. a bit expensive but was worthwhile.
Centrally located in Ahmedabad, The House of MG is a premier boutique heritage hotel in Ahmedabad, the UNESCO declared World Heritage City. One of the oldest buildings in the city, it wonderfully represents the architectural colonial heritage architecture of the early 20th century.
The hotel has 38 beautifully appointed rooms, offering all modern amenities in a grand but homely ambiance. The 20th Century family mansion that offers spacious rooms with free WiFi and a private balcony.
There is antique wooden furniture, beautiful tiled flooring, and old photographs (including one of Mahatma Gandhi) on the walls.
Ohh the unlimited gujju food and home style service with so much love like they are family! I can barely type as I'm so full. Skip the previous meal before going to Agashiye at house of MG
The best Gujarati thali I have ever had. The ambience was great and the staff very attentive and hellbent on making on feeding you till you can't have any more.

Typical and authentic local food.
Haa a legacy and very well maintained

Expensive thali at 999/- but every worth of penny spent.

Great khatiyawadi food and they serve really well.

The hotel has large rooms and is a grandeur.

Hotel building is as it was...but earlier difference was made through clients care and attention to minor points which does not exist any longer.

Now what all matters is money and milking a cow.

Since they have created a name for themselves in hotel industry, success has gone to their heads...and they are least bothered to provide services what clients have paid for.

I would not be back.
Having dinner at shiv shakti or any dhabba would have been much worth and better than having at The House of Mg. Not worth spending 2000rs Dish
Ambience is excellent. However I was disappointed cause the site said it was a vegetarian place however it served egg. It may be accepted veg by many cultures not by Indian atleast
Service is good. Food was ok. They didnt provide too much varities. But was good. Was bit expensive. Else parking space is good. Location is good. Interior and Ambience is awesome.
Love places with stories and this is a very charming one. The restaurant is great and the hotel which we had a tour of was even more exciting and enriching. Definitely staying there at the earliest.

W O W !!!...yes, a great hotel, but sooooo much more than that!!!...a museum that lives, that tells the story, that lives on and takes you with it...great to discover!...very clean, well maintained, loving with a lot Decorated with a sense of history without appearing old-fashioned but rather stimulating curiosity!...really great cinema!...the hotel itself offers great tours!...I did the Morning Heritage Walk through the Old City with Said and even though I've already done a lot After we saw it, he showed us so many more new places and, above all, told us a lot about it!...can only advise EVERY visitor to Ahmedabad to take this tour!...regardless of whether they are a house guest or not!...then the Green House!🫶🥰 😋...uncomplicated, open to everyone, warm and very L E C K E R!😋...there are local dishes, freshly prepared, they are explained and everyone can find something for themselves think!...great!...and the spa is definitely worth a visit!🥰🫶👌...super clean, hygiene and privacy are very important!...everyone is very nice and competent!
In short: it's a shame it was only 4 nights!...I would have liked to be pampered longer!...I'd love to, I'd love to do it again!🧡💛🧡

Very nice establishment, with numerous patios, the welcome is very warm.
The concierges are very attentive (help with obtaining a taxi, an address, etc.)
The swimming pool is very pretty but a little small.
Unfortunately we were expecting a buffet for breakfast, due to a lack of customers we were served at the table: only one kind of jam, very slow service, little choice, so we went outside for the other breakfasts .

Nice hotel and restaurants.  Thali's are good and wonderfully presented. Staff helpful. But you shouldn't expect a decent Western breakfast and real coffee (= powdered coffee). Furthermore, you can only eat thali's in the roof restaurant, and you must order and pay for both food and drinks in advance...

A magnificent location to visit the central market and the city. The service and attention of the staff is exquisite. And the building magnificently restored and very comfortable and comfortable. All rooms with a rocking chair. The Green House dining room is very tasty, comfortable and has very attentive waiters. The drawback for a Westerner is the noise from the street and the call to prayer from the mosque opposite. A must in Ahmehdabad.

Surely the best option if you want to be close to the old city.
The rooms are very spacious and the bed is super comfortable.
If you are one of those who need windows, confirm the visits - those on the first floor overlook a patio -

I recommend trying the thali 1400inr with fees.
There is a mosque 🕌 very close and you can hear the call to prayer. Personally it doesn't bother me, but there are people who might.

It is in a very busy area, but inside it is like an oasis.
Very friendly staff.

A historic house transformed into a hotel with two excellent restaurants. Spacious but not luxurious rooms, period furniture and well-conceived spaces in a late colonial setting. A little oasis in a city with a lot of traffic and noise that is sometimes hard not to hear.

I am a tourist from Thailand.  It's my third time going to India because I'm fascinated by the diverse culture here.  The Indian people are friendly and the food is delicious. Before going back, we went to eat at the green house restaurant which is in The House of MG. It was very delicious and we walked around and explored this place.  And intend to definitely stay here next time because it looks clean.  Shady has complete facilities, including a swimming pool. But I would like to especially praise the security guard in front of the hotel who kindly called an Auto Rickshaw (tuk tuk) for me to return to my accommodation safely.  Thank you very much.
Gorgeous historic hotel. Very clean. Rooms furnished with period furniture.
Old man. Nice rooftop restaurant. Excellent service. Large spaces. Top fruit for breakfast.
A quaint, old hotel where previous Prime Ministers of India have stayed. This is where Mahatma Gandhi stayed for a long time, and the hotel itself is a museum.

It is a very abandoned place, very dirty, the rooms smell bad, the restaurant does not have many options, the employees serve very well, but for foreign tourists it is very dirty and very deteriorated.
Opp.Amdavad heritage Sidi saiyad jali Proud of India ... proud of Gujarat.Proud feelings My Amdavad
A place worth going to at least once if you want to feel the traditions of India. Gandhi also visited.
Historic building also hotel. Great local Indian food
Very nice hotel, a great story, we’re not at Accor!
The House of MG is wonderful!
Great food and very welcoming atmosphere
Nice mansion and good food too.
Spectacular charming hotel
On

We recently had the pleasure of staying at the Hyatt Regency, and I must say it was an exceptional experience from start to finish. The staff was incredibly welcoming and attentive, making sure every aspect of my stay was comfortable and enjoyable.

The room was spacious, immaculately clean, and beautifully decorated. The bed was extremely comfortable, ensuring a restful night's sleep. I also appreciated the thoughtful amenities provided, including high-quality toiletries and a well-stocked minibar.

The hotel’s facilities are top-notch. The fitness center was well-equipped, and the pool area was a great place to relax after a day of sightseeing. I particularly enjoyed dining at the hotel restaurant; the food was delicious, and the service was impeccable.

One of the standout features of the Hyatt Regency is its location. It is conveniently situated close to major attractions, shopping areas, and public transportation, making it easy to explore the city.

Overall, my stay at the Hyatt Regency exceeded my expectations in every way. I highly recommend this hotel to anyone looking for a luxurious and comfortable

I had a very nice stay. Housekeeping needs to pull up their socks! After I put in a word it was perfect though. The staff is warm and courteous. Breakfast is sumptuous. In room dining could be a but faster! Overall value for money!

We really enjoyed our recent one week stay at Hyatt Regency Ahmedabad in October.  The room was kept very clean and had all the amenities.  Thank you to Monika who maintained our room exceptionally well.  Breakfast was great with an amazing choice.  We would definitely stay again when in Ahmedabad.

Visiting the Hyatt Regency on Ashram Road in Ahmedabad offers a luxurious and comfortable experience. The hotel’s modern design and elegant interiors create a welcoming ambiance. From the spacious, well-appointed rooms to the exceptional dining options, every aspect of the stay is geared towards relaxation and convenience. The staff’s attentive service enhances the experience, making guests feel valued. Its central location on Ashram Road also makes it convenient for exploring key attractions in the city. Overall, it’s a blend of luxury, comfort, and excellent hospitality.

Walkable distance from Sabarmati river and very close to Gandhi Ashram. Loved everything about the hotel right from welcome to checkin to check out. Hospitality of each and every staff was great. Rooms were neat and clean. Breakfast buffet was a huge spread with many options. Would love to stay again if visiting in future.

My stay at the Hyatt Regency was very pleasant. The location is perfect, close to major attractions. The riverfront view was awesome. The rooms are modern and well-maintained. Breakfast  was delectable . NEVER MISS IT. I would definitely stay here again and make sure it includes breakfast 😜🤤

By far the worst experience at a Hyatt. They delayed the check in by over an hour as they couldn't find the booking and the receptionist said they have overbooked the rooms and can't find one for us. RIVERVIEW is a joke- we didn't have any view at all. Substandard breakfast, broken toilet seat, housekeeping wouldn't clean despite several reminders. AVOID this property.

Rooms are very nice and clean, some of the items it the cheap toilet paper you associate with a public toilet not a 5 star establishment, bananas do not get replaced in the fruit bowl.
Club room drinks especially Diet Coke does not get replaced.
Even the restaurant keeps running out of Diet Coke, I have been here just 2 weeks and it runs out every other day. I know they sell them in Zepto so the problem is with the management not listening to the feedback of the staff.
Rooms are massive and bed is so comfortable. I am staying here for 1 year so hopefully I can follow up with another review to give 5 stars.

Superb hotel. Lovely hotel rooms, fantastic breakfast with so much choice for everyone.

All staff were friendly and welcoming. Brijal was very helpful during our short stay. Advised about places to go in order to get certain items we were looking for and places worth visiting, always with a smile.

Would recommend this hotel anytime. Would definitely stay again if visiting Ahmedabad.

This time we have very bad experience in this hotel for next time will find a new hotel.

If there is room dinning menu in the room and management can’t serve they why management has kept.

They cannot manage 300 people's events..A hotel can't serve 300 people, they often run out of food, and they can't manage.The staff there also treats you with a little grumpy nature.And also sarcastically looking at someone's eating style..and there were 250 to 300 people to attend the meeting and there were people from far and wide some of them very remote.  I have seen some of the hotel staff sarcastically by looking at them and seeing their eating habits.
Totally Disappointed with the service...

My recent stay at Hyatt Regency in Ahmedabad was nothing short of disappointing. They operate under the guise of five-star service, but it’s more like an open cash grab. If you're comfortable with overpaying for underwhelming and subpar services, then this place might suit you. The rates and services are entirely rigid with no consideration for customer satisfaction—just pure profit focus and  I’d strongly recommend looking elsewhere.

My stay at Hyatt Regency was disappointing from the very beginning. The photos on their website for the Regency Executive Suite are entirely misleading and do not reflect reality. From the misrepresented room photos to the indifferent service, it became clear that this hotel is far more focused on cutting costs than providing an exceptional guest experience.

The lack of basic hospitality was evident right from check-in and I had to go back and forth so many times to book a room of my choice. Post booking The staff also debated whether or not to offer a welcome drink—a courtesy you’d expect automatically when booking a suite at a "five-star" hotel. It’s baffling and frankly insulting to have to witness such hesitation; it left me questioning their commitment to customer satisfaction.

The moment I booked,  I started getting an uneasy feeling about the stay, and unfortunately, my instincts were spot on. One or Two jokers always stands near the security baggage check in space, but none of them shows any courtesy to open the hotel entry doors for the entering customers !!! Customers will have to open the doors themselves !!! What more can you expect from these morons in terms of hospitality, i am speechless?!!!

Beyond that, the amenities were seriously lacking. The air conditioning barely worked in my room, leaving the room stuffy and uncomfortable, and there was no hot water in the bathroom, which only added to the discomfort and I had to call the technicians multiple times to fix it.

The atmosphere was further dampened by mosquitoes in the lounge and seating area—a significant lapse in basic standards, let alone those expected of a five-star establishment. With so many other options in the Ashram Road area, I regret not exploring them before wasting my money here.

I had clearly mentioned during booking that I’d need a late checkout after 3 pm to have a quick lunch before leaving. However, this request wasn’t recorded, and I had to discuss it again with the front desk at checkout. Despite my prior notice, I was still charged an additional whopping INR 1800—an absolutely unrealistic figure and an avoidable hassle that showed a lack of care for guest convenience.

Despite being a returning Hyatt member, I was offered no incentives, perks or any better rates. Every little service comes with an extra charge, as if customer loyalty or satisfaction is an afterthought. I even expressed my disappointment at the front desk, and the indifferent response confirmed this was my last stay here. I won’t be returning.

Overall, it’s clear that customer satisfaction takes a back seat to profits here, and I regret choosing this property over better options nearby. I would strongly recommend looking elsewhere, especially with so many better choices nearby. This was my last stay—I won’t be returning. This place is NOT RECOMMENDED TO ANYONE...PLEASE STAY AWAY."
Visited during RBI90 Quiz. Best Hotel for the Stay and Good and Homely feel you will get. The staff is very professional and decent.

Great Hospitality,
Good food,
Great ambience,
Happy faces everywhere,
Positive environment,
With good people around you,
Each and every staff members, eager to serve his best,
Specially mentioned names, Chef Surya, Chef Pankaj.
Also Thanks, entire Hyatt Team for organizing Delicious Cake and amenities.

I had a great time at the Hyatt Regency in Ahmedabad recently. The hotel has high-quality service, luxurious living quarters, and a picturesque sight of the Sabarmati River. My stay was made very comfortable by the extremely accommodating and helpful staff of the establishment. The restaurants were varied and tasty. It is a nice place for people coming to visit either for business or pleasure.

Venue Tinello for dinner,
Sourabh from F&b service gave us attentive and smooth service, chef pankaj extended a warm welcome to us and our child. …
Great service, amazing staff, lot of options in the breakfast buffet, and close to Sabarmati Riverfront. We had an amazing stay.

Visited to book for a wedding reception.  We have liked this place for a while. However, our meeting today wasn't pleasant.  The salesperson Mr Sachin was snobby and rude and arguing about things that least mattered to this deal.  Everytime time a question was asked, he woyld either reply with how big Hyatt Regency was as a brand or he didn't have an answer.  If the sales team put half their efforts in converting business their cafe tables would be cleaner and they could afford their seats to be rid of stray hair.

Entry gate is near the rise of a beidge that is tricky. hotel is grand. Holds its Hyatt rated ambience. The day we visited it's restaurant the service was too slow. They kept asking for a free dessert as it was late. However they will surely improve on our suggestions I guess.

We are staying in a suite at the moment with a bath tub  and the water is luke warm and they refuse to turn up the temperature - whilst my sister in law down the corridor has hot water we do not. Very disappointed.

Great service during the meals by Vishrut and Kundan.

Amrish was really amazing, understanding and remembered our preferences for the entire week. He always greeted us with a smile and made our meals worthwhile during the stay.

I consistently return to the Hyatt Regency Ahmedabad for its exceptional dining experience, which is the primary reason I keep renewing my HDC membership. The ambience, attentive staff, and prime location create a welcoming atmosphere that I truly appreciate. Mr. Kapil, Mr. Sanjay, Seba, Dharmesh, Philomena and their front desk team, along with Abhishek Rana,Sangeeta, Chef Surya, Chef Pankaj, and GM Mr. Punitji, consistently go above and beyond to ensure a warm greeting and a comfortable experience.

Breakfast is one of my favorite parts of my stay, offering a delightful start to the day with a diverse and delectable menu. Whether it's breakfast, lunch, or dinner, the culinary journey feels like a refreshing escape, setting a positive tone for the rest of my day. Additionally, the spa is a highlight of the hotel, providing a perfect opportunity for relaxation. I also recommend booking a river-view room, which makes your stay beautifully peaceful.

This was my first stay with Hyatt In Ahmedabad, it was above expectations.
Didn’t though to get such an amazing service and hospitality in Ahmedabad.
Will definitely repeat my stay whenever I’m back. Very kind and helpful staff, there was a girl Philomina in restaurant, she was helping my mom while she was in restaurant, it was very satisfying.
Thanks guys for everything. Enjoyed my stay.

I recently stayed at this property with my family, having stayed at multiple Hyatt locations before, but this experience was notably different. Unfortunately, the staff here were the rudest I've encountered among all the top-rated hotels I've visited.

There were several unpleasant encounters, but one that stood out involved breakfast. My mum found a hair in her paratha while chewing it, and we discreetly informed the nearest staff member, not wanting to make a big scene. However, a manager in a blue suit approached us and, rather than addressing the issue, claimed the hair belonged to my mother since they had no female staff. He calmly shifted the blame onto us and then left.

This response was extremely disappointing, and it left a sour impression on what should have been a pleasant stay.

Lovely stay. Had lots of fun. Had a great great time. Perfect service, up to mark ambience. Love the hospitality. Love the food. Chefs are super friendly and knowledgeable. Room service is up to date. The pool is lovely.

The Hyatt Regency Ahmedabad offers spacious and well-maintained rooms, featuring modern amenities and unique bathroom designs, ensuring a comfortable stay for guests.
Service is a standout aspect, with many reviews praising the courteous and attentive staff, particularly in room service, which is noted for its quality despite being on the pricier side.
Located conveniently, the hotel provides easy access to local attractions, making it an ideal choice for both business and leisure travelers seeking luxury and convenience in Ahmedabad.

I visited Tinello, the all-Italian restaurant at Hyatt Regency. The ambiance was pleasant, and the staff was friendly. While the food was generally okay, the chicken had an aftertaste reminiscent of chlorine. However, the mocktails were refreshing, and the spaghetti and Pasta  was delicious.

The worst experience ever, with the worst service and rooms. This will be my first and last stay at any Hyatt Regency in the world. The reception staff were rude and liars. I strongly advise against staying here if you want a pleasant and respectful experience. Avoid this hotel at all costs.

It is “fine” for a hotel. The club level comes with no ability to use the terrace or have any of the windows open during sunset. I’d recommend another hotel while staying in Ahmedabad. If you’re going to build a hotel on a river, ensure there are views of the city that can be enjoyed by guests.

Hi,
Did a late Check-in post 12am today and to my surprise continuous dripping of water from the AC outlet. Room 338 - Hyatt Regency - Ashram Road. Thank fully will be checking out in the morning. This is the first and the last time staying at the hotel.

I visited the Hyatt Regency for an Investor Fair organized by NSE and ANMI. The location is convenient, being close to the railway station. The food served was of good quality with a nice variety. The hall was spacious, making it a great venue for events like this.

Typical 4/5 star hotel in heart of Ahmedabad on ashram road m some rooms offer partial view of the Sabarmati river front

Property is a bit dated but maintenance was good .

The river front garden a short walk away provides great opportunity for a walk and workout

Oswal a legendary restaurant for fafda and  gota , a gujarati delicacy is very close by
Fantastic experience. I spent two days at the property. The food is top notch. Nothing to complain about. I loved the breads, cakes and cinnamon rolls.

I'm going to be direct - the room we were given was far below the Hyatt standard we expected.

The table was scratched up, the sofa chair had stains, and the pillows looked dingy. And the TV? A shitty little thing without even HD channels. This is not what I booked or what I would expect from a Hyatt property.

After wasting time changing rooms twice, we finally settled on a room we just had to accept, even though the AC was set to 18 degrees but still felt warm. It was late and we just wanted to go to bed at that point.

This stay was a real letdown, and not at all the Hyatt experience I'm used to. I expected a lot more from your brand.
A good 5.star hotel well located on the ashram road.  Good place for stay for visiting professionals in the city who have work at both gnadhinagar and Ahmedabad.
I’m stay at Hyatt at Amedabad. The services are very nice. This morning Abhishek help and take care of my table. His service is very impressive.

Best food preparation..veg sabji and the special khichadi made by Chef Bhuwan was really amazing ..I just rate this more than 5 star ..Hyatt residency buffet is definitely to try for .felt like second home
Very cozy ambiance at the Chai Shop, great service experience with Mr. Gautam. Ordered some custom Indian sandwiches off menu, they happily made it for us.
One of the best hotel near by Ashram road, food is amazing even banquet

Excellent food at The Chai Shop! I love their selection of tea and coffee. Tanvi is super amicable and efficient. Many thanks for a pleasant evening!
An unforgettable dining experience with exceptional service. The staff was too polite and cooperative especially Mr Vishrut and Mr Amrish.

One of the best resturant experience! The food was nice! Mr Kundan and Ms Tanvi were extremely helpful!!
Well mannered staff, shifa organised family to be all seated at same table for breakfast each morning. Good

Best Italian restaurant in Ahmedabad!!!
Great food, Amazing ambiance and such a great staff especially Mr Amrish..A must visit
Hotel Hyatt is truly incredible and unbelievable! From the luxurious rooms to the top-notch service, it's a place where you can relax, recharge, and feel empowered.

Its a scam for room category here too.... Even its a good brand i will not be comfortable to stay here again

The restaurant was great. Had a server named Neel who was really great aswell.
The foods amazing.

Attended event by Linkedin. Just saw mouse in the hall (on wall behind screen) where event was there, so just check that part. Food, ambience, premise, valet parking exp was good. Keep working and improving.
Had to wait for a while for breakfast. Otherwise the stay was quite comfy in their spacious room.
Nice business hotel . Clean rooms with all basic amenities great spa and food is very good try gujurati thali
Enjoyed the stay here. Spacious rooms & bathrooms. Attentive staff, Good place to stay with kids. clean.

This hotel I fount has the worst management, we had event in the hotel, I left my charger in the hall and no body took the responsibility to help me allocate, rather they said not found. This is a basic, luxury business hotel. Has all the amenities. Nothing super fancy or extravagant. Found the staff to be courteous and kind but I guess lacked some basic knowledge. The breakfast was great and the room view of the river was spectacular. If I come on a business trip to Ahmedabad again, I will stay here.

Hyatt Regency Ahmedabad is such a pleasant surprise. I have stayed in other hotels in Abad but Hyatt regency is super professional in every part. I am a difficult guest as I have certain choices on room number, preferences on food, demands on in room dining .. but all of it was awesomely handled. Parag Shidore ensured I got my room number even when I checked in very late in the evening. Akash Sharma the restaurant manager was superb and always helpful at all times. Helped me arrange a cake at very short notice. The breakfast spread is AMAZING !!!Ashish from in room dining was amazing. The spa is beautifully managed and so are the housekeeping services.
I feel this is my to go hotel now onwards everytime I come to Ahmedabad. Definitely a 5 star in it's true worth !

What a beautiful experience. Such a beautiful set of people working together to give you a positive experience. Want to mention Prajakta who from the reception really made my trip smooth and efficient. Hope to stay with you folks soon!
We had meeting at Hyatt regency.poor disappointed by service.👎There is no differebt kitchen for veg and non veg dish.both dished made on same counter.Non vej maker person touch veg dishes also. …
Rooms and Meeting Hall is too good but food taste is average. Service Location is Wonderful.
we did many meetings here, ambiance is very good, service and food is very good. enough parking you can get.

Visited this hotel recently and had a fantastic experience! The food was delicious, the view was breathtaking, and the service was top-notch. The prices were reasonable too. Overall, a perfect stay. Highly recommend! Additionally, the ambiance was inviting, and the staff was exceptionally friendly and accommodating.
We have been to Tinello for best service and food for so long it just doesn’t seem to disappoint ever… must come
